verses.kjv: NOW it came to pass after the death of Saul, when David was returned from the slaughter of the Amalekites, and David had abode two days in Ziklag;
verses.bsb: After the death of Saul, David returned from the slaughter of the Amalekites and stayed in Ziklag two days.

verses.kjv: It came even to pass on the third day, that, behold, a man came out of the camp from Saul with his clothes rent, and earth upon his head: and so it was, when he came to David, that he fell to the earth, and did obeisance.
verses.bsb: On the third day a man with torn clothes and dust on his head arrived from Saul’s camp. When he came to David, he fell to the ground to pay him homage.

verses.kjv: And David said unto him, From whence comest thou? And he said unto him, Out of the camp of Israel am I escaped.
verses.bsb: “Where have you come from?” David asked.  “I have escaped from the Israelite camp,” he replied.

verses.kjv: And David said unto him, How went the matter? I pray thee, tell me. And he answered, That the people are fled from the battle, and many of the people also are fallen and dead; and Saul and Jonathan his son are dead also.
verses.bsb: “What was the outcome?” David asked. “Please tell me.”  “The troops fled from the battle,” he replied. “Many of them fell and died. And Saul and his son Jonathan are also dead.”

verses.kjv: And David said unto the young man that told him, How knowest thou that Saul and Jonathan his son be dead?
verses.bsb: Then David asked the young man who had brought him the report, “How do you know that Saul and his son Jonathan are dead?”

verses.kjv: And the young man that told him said, As I happened by chance upon mount Gilboa, behold, Saul leaned upon his spear; and, lo, the chariots and horsemen followed hard after him.
verses.bsb: “I happened to be on Mount Gilboa,” he replied, “and there was Saul, leaning on his spear, with the chariots and the cavalry closing in on him.

verses.kjv: And when he looked behind him, he saw me, and called unto me. And I answered, Here am I.
verses.bsb: When he turned around and saw me, he called out and I answered, ‘Here I am!’

verses.kjv: And he said unto me, Who art thou? And I answered him, I am an Amalekite.
verses.bsb: ‘Who are you?’ he asked.  So I told him, ‘I am an Amalekite.’

verses.kjv: He said unto me again, Stand, I pray thee, upon me, and slay me: for anguish is come upon me, because my life is yet whole in me.
verses.bsb: Then he begged me, ‘Stand over me and kill me, for agony has seized me, but my life still lingers.’

verses.kjv: So I stood upon him, and slew him, because I was sure that he could not live after that he was fallen: and I took the crown that was upon his head, and the bracelet that was on his arm, and have brought them hither unto my lord.
verses.bsb: So I stood over him and killed him, because I knew that after he had fallen he could not survive. And I took the crown that was on his head and the band that was on his arm, and I have brought them here to my lord.”

verses.kjv: Then David took hold on his clothes, and rent them; and likewise all the men that were with him:
verses.bsb: Then David took hold of his own clothes and tore them, and all the men who were with him did the same.

verses.kjv: And they mourned, and wept, and fasted until even, for Saul, and for Jonathan his son, and for the people of the Lord, and for the house of Israel; because they were fallen by the sword.
verses.bsb: They mourned and wept and fasted until evening for Saul and his son Jonathan, and for the people of the LORD and the house of Israel, because they had fallen by the sword.

verses.kjv: ¶ And David said unto the young man that told him, Whence art thou? And he answered, I am the son of a stranger, an Amalekite.
verses.bsb: And David inquired of the young man who had brought him the report, “Where are you from?”  “I am the son of a foreigner,” he answered. “I am an Amalekite.”

verses.kjv: And David said unto him, How wast thou not afraid to stretch forth thine hand to destroy the Lord’s anointed?
verses.bsb: So David asked him, “Why were you not afraid to lift your hand to destroy the LORD’s anointed?”

verses.kjv: And David called one of the young men, and said, Go near, and fall upon him. And he smote him that he died.
verses.bsb: Then David summoned one of the young men and said, “Go, execute him!” So the young man struck him down, and he died.

verses.kjv: And David said unto him, Thy blood be upon thy head; for thy mouth hath testified against thee, saying, I have slain the Lord’s anointed.
verses.bsb: For David had said to the Amalekite, “Your blood be on your own head because your own mouth has testified against you, saying, ‘I killed the LORD’s anointed.’”

verses.kjv: ¶ And David lamented with this lamentation over Saul and over Jonathan his son:
verses.bsb: Then David took up this lament for Saul and his son Jonathan,

verses.kjv: (Also he bade them teach the children of Judah the use of the bow: behold, it is written in the book of Jasher.)
verses.bsb: and he ordered that the sons of Judah be taught the Song of the Bow. It is written in the Book of Jashar:

verses.kjv: The beauty of Israel is slain upon thy high places: how are the mighty fallen!
verses.bsb: “Your glory, O Israel, lies slain on your heights. How the mighty have fallen!

verses.kjv: Tell it not in Gath, publish it not in the streets of Askelon; lest the daughters of the Philistines rejoice, lest the daughters of the uncircumcised triumph.
verses.bsb: Tell it not in Gath; proclaim it not in the streets of Ashkelon, lest the daughters of the Philistines rejoice, and the daughters of the uncircumcised exult.

verses.kjv: Ye mountains of Gilboa, let there be no dew, neither let there be rain, upon you, nor fields of offerings: for there the shield of the mighty is vilely cast away, the shield of Saul, as though he had not been anointed with oil.
verses.bsb: O mountains of Gilboa, may you have no dew or rain, no fields yielding offerings of grain. For there the shield of the mighty was defiled, the shield of Saul, no longer anointed with oil.

verses.kjv: From the blood of the slain, from the fat of the mighty, the bow of Jonathan turned not back, and the sword of Saul returned not empty.
verses.bsb: From the blood of the slain, from the fat of the mighty, the bow of Jonathan did not retreat, and the sword of Saul did not return empty.

verses.kjv: Saul and Jonathan were lovely and pleasant in their lives, and in their death they were not divided: they were swifter than eagles, they were stronger than lions.
verses.bsb: Saul and Jonathan, beloved and delightful in life, were not divided in death. They were swifter than eagles, they were stronger than lions.

verses.kjv: Ye daughters of Israel, weep over Saul, who clothed you in scarlet, with other delights, who put on ornaments of gold upon your apparel.
verses.bsb: O daughters of Israel, weep for Saul, who clothed you in scarlet and luxury, who decked your garments with ornaments of gold.

verses.kjv: How are the mighty fallen in the midst of the battle! O Jonathan, thou wast slain in thine high places.
verses.bsb: How the mighty have fallen in the thick of battle! Jonathan lies slain on your heights.

verses.kjv: I am distressed for thee, my brother Jonathan: very pleasant hast thou been unto me: thy love to me was wonderful, passing the love of women.
verses.bsb: I grieve for you, Jonathan, my brother. You were delightful to me; your love to me was extraordinary, surpassing the love of women.

verses.kjv: How are the mighty fallen, and the weapons of war perished!
verses.bsb: How the mighty have fallen and the weapons of war have perished!”

verses.kjv: AND it came to pass after this, that David inquired of the Lord, saying, Shall I go up into any of the cities of Judah? And the Lord said unto him, Go up. And David said, Whither shall I go up? And he said, Unto Hebron.
verses.bsb: Some time later, David inquired of the LORD, “Should I go up to one of the towns of Judah?”  “Go up,” the LORD answered.  Then David asked, “Where should I go?”  “To Hebron,” replied the LORD.

verses.kjv: So David went up thither, and his two wives also, Ahinoam the Jezreelitess, and Abigail Nabal’s wife the Carmelite.
verses.bsb: So David went there with his two wives, Ahinoam of Jezreel and Abigail the widow of Nabal of Carmel.

verses.kjv: And his men that were with him did David bring up, every man with his household: and they dwelt in the cities of Hebron.
verses.bsb: David also took the men who were with him, each with his household, and they settled in the towns near Hebron.

verses.kjv: And the men of Judah came, and there they anointed David king over the house of Judah. And they told David, saying, That the men of Jabesh-gilead were they that buried Saul.
verses.bsb: Then the men of Judah came to Hebron, and there they anointed David king over the house of Judah. And they told David, “It was the men of Jabesh-gilead who buried Saul.”

verses.kjv: ¶ And David sent messengers unto the men of Jabesh-gilead, and said unto them, Blessed be ye of the Lord, that ye have shewed this kindness unto your lord, even unto Saul, and have buried him.
verses.bsb: So David sent messengers to the men of Jabesh-gilead to tell them, “The LORD bless you, because you showed this kindness to Saul your lord when you buried him.

verses.kjv: And now the Lord shew kindness and truth unto you: and I also will requite you this kindness, because ye have done this thing.
verses.bsb: Now may the LORD show you loving devotion and faithfulness, and I will also show you the same favor because you have done this.

verses.kjv: Therefore now let your hands be strengthened, and be ye valiant: for your master Saul is dead, and also the house of Judah have anointed me king over them.
verses.bsb: Now then, be strong and courageous, for though Saul your lord is dead, the house of Judah has anointed me as their king.”

verses.kjv: ¶ But Abner the son of Ner, captain of Saul’s host, took Ish-bosheth the son of Saul, and brought him over to Mahanaim;
verses.bsb: Meanwhile, Abner son of Ner, the commander of Saul’s army, took Saul’s son Ish-bosheth, moved him to Mahanaim,

verses.kjv: And made him king over Gilead, and over the Ashurites, and over Jezreel, and over Ephraim, and over Benjamin, and over all Israel.
verses.bsb: and made him king over Gilead, Asher, Jezreel, Ephraim, and Benjamin—over all Israel.

verses.kjv: Ish-bosheth Saul’s son was forty years old when he began to reign over Israel, and reigned two years. But the house of Judah followed David.
verses.bsb: Saul’s son Ish-bosheth was forty years old when he began to reign over Israel, and he reigned for two years.  The house of Judah, however, followed David.

verses.kjv: And the time that David was king in Hebron over the house of Judah was seven years and six months.
verses.bsb: And the length of time that David was king in Hebron over the house of Judah was seven years and six months.

verses.kjv: ¶ And Abner the son of Ner, and the servants of Ish-bosheth the son of Saul, went out from Mahanaim to Gibeon.
verses.bsb: One day Abner son of Ner and the servants of Ish-bosheth son of Saul marched out from Mahanaim to Gibeon.

verses.kjv: And Joab the son of Zeruiah, and the servants of David, went out, and met together by the pool of Gibeon: and they sat down, the one on the one side of the pool, and the other on the other side of the pool.
verses.bsb: So Joab son of Zeruiah, along with the servants of David, marched out and met them by the pool of Gibeon. And the two camps took up positions on opposite sides of the pool.

verses.kjv: And Abner said to Joab, Let the young men now arise, and play before us. And Joab said, Let them arise.
verses.bsb: Then Abner said to Joab, “Let us have the young men get up and compete before us.”  “Let them get up,” Joab replied.

verses.kjv: Then there arose and went over by number twelve of Benjamin, which pertained to Ish-bosheth the son of Saul, and twelve of the servants of David.
verses.bsb: So they got up and were counted off—twelve for Benjamin and Ish-bosheth son of Saul, and twelve for David.

verses.kjv: And they caught every one his fellow by the head, and thrust his sword in his fellow’s side; so they fell down together: wherefore that place was called Helkath-hazzurim, which is in Gibeon.
verses.bsb: Then each man grabbed his opponent by the head and thrust his sword into his opponent’s side, and they all fell together. So this place, which is in Gibeon, is called Helkath-hazzurim.

verses.kjv: And there was a very sore battle that day; and Abner was beaten, and the men of Israel, before the servants of David.
verses.bsb: The battle that day was intense, and Abner and the men of Israel were defeated by the servants of David.

verses.kjv: ¶ And there were three sons of Zeruiah there, Joab, and Abishai, and Asahel: and Asahel was as light of foot as a wild roe.
verses.bsb: The three sons of Zeruiah were there: Joab, Abishai, and Asahel. Now Asahel was fleet of foot like a wild gazelle,

verses.kjv: And Asahel pursued after Abner; and in going he turned not to the right hand nor to the left from following Abner.
verses.bsb: and he chased Abner, not turning to the right or to the left in his pursuit.

verses.kjv: Then Abner looked behind him, and said, Art thou Asahel? And he answered, I am.
verses.bsb: And Abner glanced back and said, “Is that you, Asahel?”  “It is,” Asahel replied.

verses.kjv: And Abner said to him, Turn thee aside to thy right hand or to thy left, and lay thee hold on one of the young men, and take thee his armour. But Asahel would not turn aside from following of him.
verses.bsb: So Abner told him, “Turn to your right or to your left, seize one of the young men, and take his equipment for yourself.”  But Asahel would not stop chasing him.

verses.kjv: And Abner said again to Asahel, Turn thee aside from following me: wherefore should I smite thee to the ground? how then should I hold up my face to Joab thy brother?
verses.bsb: Once again, Abner warned Asahel, “Stop chasing me. Why should I strike you to the ground? How could I show my face to your brother Joab?”

verses.kjv: Howbeit he refused to turn aside: wherefore Abner with the hinder end of the spear smote him under the fifth rib, that the spear came out behind him; and he fell down there, and died in the same place: and it came to pass, that as many as came to the place where Asahel fell down and died stood still.
verses.bsb: But Asahel refused to turn away; so Abner thrust the butt of his spear into his stomach, and it came out his back, and he fell dead on the spot. And every man paused when he came to the place where Asahel had fallen and died.

verses.kjv: Joab also and Abishai pursued after Abner: and the sun went down when they were come to the hill of Ammah, that lieth before Giah by the way of the wilderness of Gibeon.
verses.bsb: But Joab and Abishai pursued Abner. By sunset, they had gone as far as the hill of Ammah opposite Giah on the way to the wilderness of Gibeon.

verses.kjv: ¶ And the children of Benjamin gathered themselves together after Abner, and became one troop, and stood on the top of an hill.
verses.bsb: The Benjamites rallied to Abner, formed a single unit, and took their stand atop a hill.

verses.kjv: Then Abner called to Joab, and said, Shall the sword devour for ever? knowest thou not that it will be bitterness in the latter end? how long shall it be then, ere thou bid the people return from following their brethren?
verses.bsb: Then Abner called out to Joab: “Must the sword devour forever? Do you not realize that this will only end in bitterness? How long before you tell the troops to stop pursuing their brothers?”

verses.kjv: And Joab said, As God liveth, unless thou hadst spoken, surely then in the morning the people had gone up every one from following his brother.
verses.bsb: “As surely as God lives,” Joab replied, “if you had not spoken up, the troops would have continued pursuing their brothers until morning.”

verses.kjv: So Joab blew a trumpet, and all the people stood still, and pursued after Israel no more, neither fought they any more.
verses.bsb: So Joab blew the ram’s horn, and all the troops stopped; they no longer pursued Israel or continued to fight.

verses.kjv: And Abner and his men walked all that night through the plain, and passed over Jordan, and went through all Bithron, and they came to Mahanaim.
verses.bsb: And all that night Abner and his men marched through the Arabah. They crossed the Jordan, marched all morning, and arrived at Mahanaim.

verses.kjv: And Joab returned from following Abner: and when he had gathered all the people together, there lacked of David’s servants nineteen men and Asahel.
verses.bsb: When Joab returned from pursuing Abner, he gathered all the troops.  In addition to Asahel, nineteen of David’s servants were missing,

verses.kjv: But the servants of David had smitten of Benjamin, and of Abner’s men, so that three hundred and threescore men died.
verses.bsb: but they had struck down 360 Benjamites who were with Abner.

verses.kjv: ¶ And they took up Asahel, and buried him in the sepulchre of his father, which was in Beth-lehem. And Joab and his men went all night, and they came to Hebron at break of day.
verses.bsb: Later, they took Asahel and buried him in his father’s tomb in Bethlehem. Then Joab and his men marched all night and reached Hebron at daybreak.

verses.kjv: NOW there was long war between the house of Saul and the house of David: but David waxed stronger and stronger, and the house of Saul waxed weaker and weaker.
verses.bsb: Now the war between the house of Saul and the house of David was protracted. And David grew stronger and stronger, while the house of Saul grew weaker and weaker.

verses.kjv: ¶ And unto David were sons born in Hebron: and his firstborn was Amnon, of Ahinoam the Jezreelitess;
verses.bsb: And sons were born to David in Hebron:  His firstborn was Amnon, by Ahinoam of Jezreel;

verses.kjv: And his second, Chileab, of Abigail the wife of Nabal the Carmelite; and the third, Absalom the son of Maacah the daughter of Talmai king of Geshur;
verses.bsb: his second was Chileab, by Abigail the widow of Nabal of Carmel;   his third was Absalom, the son of Maacah daughter of King Talmai of Geshur;

verses.kjv: And the fourth, Adonijah the son of Haggith; and the fifth, Shephatiah the son of Abital;
verses.bsb: his fourth was Adonijah, the son of Haggith;   his fifth was Shephatiah, the son of Abital;

verses.kjv: And the sixth, Ithream, by Eglah David’s wife. These were born to David in Hebron.
verses.bsb: and his sixth was Ithream, by David’s wife Eglah.   These sons were born to David in Hebron.

verses.kjv: ¶ And it came to pass, while there was war between the house of Saul and the house of David, that Abner made himself strong for the house of Saul.
verses.bsb: During the war between the house of Saul and the house of David, Abner had continued to strengthen his position in the house of Saul.

verses.kjv: And Saul had a concubine, whose name was Rizpah, the daughter of Aiah: and Ish-bosheth said to Abner, Wherefore hast thou gone in unto my father’s concubine?
verses.bsb: Meanwhile, Saul had a concubine named Rizpah, the daughter of Aiah. So Ish-bosheth questioned Abner, “Why did you sleep with my father’s concubine?”

verses.kjv: Then was Abner very wroth for the words of Ish-bosheth, and said, Am I a dog’s head, which against Judah do shew kindness this day unto the house of Saul thy father, to his brethren, and to his friends, and have not delivered thee into the hand of David, that thou chargest me to day with a fault concerning this woman?
verses.bsb: Abner was furious over Ish-bosheth’s accusation. “Am I the head of a dog that belongs to Judah?” he asked. “All this time I have been loyal to the house of your father Saul, to his brothers, and to his friends. I have not delivered you into the hand of David, but now you accuse me of wrongdoing with this woman!

verses.kjv: So do God to Abner, and more also, except, as the Lord hath sworn to David, even so I do to him;
verses.bsb: May God punish Abner, and ever so severely, if I do not do for David what the LORD has sworn to him:

verses.kjv: To translate the kingdom from the house of Saul, and to set up the throne of David over Israel and over Judah, from Dan even to Beer-sheba.
verses.bsb: to transfer the kingdom from the house of Saul and to establish the throne of David over Israel and Judah, from Dan to Beersheba.”

verses.kjv: And he could not answer Abner a word again, because he feared him.
verses.bsb: And for fear of Abner, Ish-bosheth did not dare to say another word to him.

verses.kjv: ¶ And Abner sent messengers to David on his behalf, saying, Whose is the land? saying also, Make thy league with me, and, behold, my hand shall be with thee, to bring about all Israel unto thee.
verses.bsb: Then Abner sent messengers in his place to say to David, “To whom does the land belong? Make your covenant with me, and surely my hand will be with you to bring all Israel over to you.”

verses.kjv: ¶ And he said, Well; I will make a league with thee: but one thing I require of thee, that is, Thou shalt not see my face, except thou first bring Michal Saul’s daughter, when thou comest to see my face.
verses.bsb: “Good,” replied David, “I will make a covenant with you. But there is one thing I require of you: Do not appear before me unless you bring Saul’s daughter Michal when you come to see me.”

verses.kjv: And David sent messengers to Ish-bosheth Saul’s son, saying, Deliver me my wife Michal, which I espoused to me for an hundred foreskins of the Philistines.
verses.bsb: Then David sent messengers to say to Ish-bosheth son of Saul, “Give me back my wife, Michal, whom I betrothed to myself for a hundred Philistine foreskins.”

verses.kjv: And Ish-bosheth sent, and took her from her husband, even from Phaltiel the son of Laish.
verses.bsb: So Ish-bosheth sent and took Michal from her husband Paltiel son of Laish.

verses.kjv: And her husband went with her along weeping behind her to Bahurim. Then said Abner unto him, Go, return. And he returned.
verses.bsb: Her husband followed her, weeping all the way to Bahurim. Then Abner said to him, “Go back.” So he returned home.

verses.kjv: ¶ And Abner had communication with the elders of Israel, saying, Ye sought for David in times past to be king over you:
verses.bsb: Now Abner conferred with the elders of Israel and said, “In the past you sought David as your king.

verses.kjv: Now then do it: for the Lord hath spoken of David, saying, By the hand of my servant David I will save my people Israel out of the hand of the Philistines, and out of the hand of all their enemies.
verses.bsb: Now take action, because the LORD has said to David, ‘Through My servant David I will save My people Israel from the hands of the Philistines and of all their enemies.’”

verses.kjv: And Abner also spake in the ears of Benjamin: and Abner went also to speak in the ears of David in Hebron all that seemed good to Israel, and that seemed good to the whole house of Benjamin.
verses.bsb: Abner also spoke to the Benjamites and went to Hebron to tell David all that seemed good to Israel and to the whole house of Benjamin.

verses.kjv: So Abner came to David to Hebron, and twenty men with him. And David made Abner and the men that were with him a feast.
verses.bsb: When Abner and twenty of his men came to David at Hebron, David held a feast for them.

verses.kjv: And Abner said unto David, I will arise and go, and will gather all Israel unto my lord the king, that they may make a league with thee, and that thou mayest reign over all that thine heart desireth. And David sent Abner away; and he went in peace.
verses.bsb: Then Abner said to David, “Let me go at once, and I will gather all Israel to my lord the king, that they may make a covenant with you, and that you may rule over all that your heart desires.”  So David dismissed Abner, and he went in peace.

verses.kjv: ¶ And, behold, the servants of David and Joab came from pursuing a troop, and brought in a great spoil with them: but Abner was not with David in Hebron; for he had sent him away, and he was gone in peace.
verses.bsb: Just then David’s soldiers and Joab returned from a raid, bringing with them a great plunder. But Abner was not with David in Hebron because David had sent him on his way in peace.

verses.kjv: When Joab and all the host that was with him were come, they told Joab, saying, Abner the son of Ner came to the king, and he hath sent him away, and he is gone in peace.
verses.bsb: When Joab and all his troops arrived, he was informed, “Abner son of Ner came to see the king, who sent him on his way in peace.”

verses.kjv: Then Joab came to the king, and said, What hast thou done? behold, Abner came unto thee; why is it that thou hast sent him away, and he is quite gone?
verses.bsb: So Joab went to the king and said, “What have you done? Look, Abner came to you. Why did you dismiss him? Now he is getting away!

verses.kjv: Thou knowest Abner the son of Ner, that he came to deceive thee, and to know thy going out and thy coming in, and to know all that thou doest.
verses.bsb: Surely you realize that Abner son of Ner came to deceive you and to track your movements and all that you are doing.”

verses.kjv: And when Joab was come out from David, he sent messengers after Abner, which brought him again from the well of Sirah: but David knew it not.
verses.bsb: As soon as Joab had left David, he sent messengers after Abner, who brought him back from the well of Sirah; but David was unaware of it.

verses.kjv: And when Abner was returned to Hebron, Joab took him aside in the gate to speak with him quietly, and smote him there under the fifth rib, that he died, for the blood of Asahel his brother.
verses.bsb: When Abner returned to Hebron, Joab pulled him aside into the gateway, as if to speak to him privately, and there Joab stabbed him in the stomach. So Abner died on account of the blood of Joab’s brother Asahel.

verses.kjv: ¶ And afterward when David heard it, he said, I and my kingdom are guiltless before the Lord for ever from the blood of Abner the son of Ner:
verses.bsb: Afterward, David heard about this and said, “I and my kingdom are forever guiltless before the LORD concerning the blood of Abner son of Ner.

verses.kjv: Let it rest on the head of Joab, and on all his father’s house; and let there not fail from the house of Joab one that hath an issue, or that is a leper, or that leaneth on a staff, or that falleth on the sword, or that lacketh bread.
verses.bsb: May it whirl over the heads of Joab and the entire house of his father, and may the house of Joab never be without one having a discharge or skin disease, or one who leans on a staff or falls by the sword or lacks food.”

verses.kjv: So Joab and Abishai his brother slew Abner, because he had slain their brother Asahel at Gibeon in the battle.
verses.bsb: (Joab and his brother Abishai murdered Abner because he had killed their brother Asahel in the battle at Gibeon.)

verses.kjv: ¶ And David said to Joab, and to all the people that were with him, Rend your clothes, and gird you with sackcloth, and mourn before Abner. And king David himself followed the bier.
verses.bsb: Then David ordered Joab and all the people with him, “Tear your clothes, put on sackcloth, and mourn before Abner.” And King David himself walked behind the funeral bier.

verses.kjv: And they buried Abner in Hebron: and the king lifted up his voice, and wept at the grave of Abner; and all the people wept.
verses.bsb: When they buried Abner in Hebron, the king wept aloud at Abner’s tomb, and all the people wept.

verses.kjv: And the king lamented over Abner, and said, Died Abner as a fool dieth?
verses.bsb: And the king sang this lament for Abner:  “Should Abner die the death of a fool?

verses.kjv: Thy hands were not bound, nor thy feet put into fetters: as a man falleth before wicked men, so fellest thou. And all the people wept again over him.
verses.bsb: Your hands were not bound, your feet were not fettered. As a man falls before the wicked, so also you fell.”   And all the people wept over him even more.

verses.kjv: And when all the people came to cause David to eat meat while it was yet day, David sware, saying, So do God to me, and more also, if I taste bread, or ought else, till the sun be down.
verses.bsb: Then all the people came and urged David to eat something while it was still day, but David took an oath, saying, “May God punish me, and ever so severely, if I taste bread or anything else before the sun sets!”

verses.kjv: And all the people took notice of it, and it pleased them: as whatsoever the king did pleased all the people.
verses.bsb: All the people took note and were pleased. In fact, everything the king did pleased them.

verses.kjv: For all the people and all Israel understood that day that it was not of the king to slay Abner the son of Ner.
verses.bsb: So on that day all the troops and all Israel were convinced that the king had no part in the murder of Abner son of Ner.

verses.kjv: And the king said unto his servants, Know ye not that there is a prince and a great man fallen this day in Israel?
verses.bsb: Then the king said to his servants, “Do you not realize that a great prince has fallen today in Israel?

verses.kjv: And I am this day weak, though anointed king; and these men the sons of Zeruiah be too hard for me: the Lord shall reward the doer of evil according to his wickedness.
verses.bsb: And I am weak this day, though anointed as king, and these men, the sons of Zeruiah, are too fierce for me. May the LORD repay the evildoer according to his evil!”

verses.kjv: AND when Saul’s son heard that Abner was dead in Hebron, his hands were feeble, and all the Israelites were troubled.
verses.bsb: Now when Ish-bosheth son of Saul heard that Abner had died in Hebron, he lost courage, and all Israel was dismayed.

verses.kjv: And Saul’s son had two men that were captains of bands: the name of the one was Baanah, and the name of the other Rechab, the sons of Rimmon a Beerothite, of the children of Benjamin: (for Beeroth also was reckoned to Benjamin:
verses.bsb: Saul’s son had two men who were leaders of raiding parties. One was named Baanah and the other Rechab; they were sons of Rimmon the Beerothite of the tribe of Benjamin—Beeroth is considered part of Benjamin,

verses.kjv: And the Beerothites fled to Gittaim, and were sojourners there until this day.)
verses.bsb: because the Beerothites fled to Gittaim and have lived there as foreigners to this day.

verses.kjv: And Jonathan, Saul’s son, had a son that was lame of his feet. He was five years old when the tidings came of Saul and Jonathan out of Jezreel, and his nurse took him up, and fled: and it came to pass, as she made haste to flee, that he fell, and became lame. And his name was Mephibosheth.
verses.bsb: And Jonathan son of Saul had a son who was lame in his feet. He was five years old when the report about Saul and Jonathan came from Jezreel. His nurse picked him up and fled, but as she was hurrying to escape, he fell and became lame. His name was Mephibosheth.

verses.kjv: And the sons of Rimmon the Beerothite, Rechab and Baanah, went, and came about the heat of the day to the house of Ish-bosheth, who lay on a bed at noon.
verses.bsb: Now Rechab and Baanah, the sons of Rimmon the Beerothite, set out and arrived at the house of Ish-bosheth in the heat of the day, while the king was taking his midday nap.

verses.kjv: And they came thither into the midst of the house, as though they would have fetched wheat; and they smote him under the fifth rib: and Rechab and Baanah his brother escaped.
verses.bsb: They entered the interior of the house as if to get some wheat, and they stabbed him in the stomach. Then Rechab and his brother Baanah slipped away.

verses.kjv: For when they came into the house, he lay on his bed in his bedchamber, and they smote him, and slew him, and beheaded him, and took his head, and gat them away through the plain all night.
verses.bsb: They had entered the house while Ish-bosheth was lying on his bed, and having stabbed and killed him, they beheaded him, took his head, and traveled all night by way of the Arabah.

verses.kjv: And they brought the head of Ish-bosheth unto David to Hebron, and said to the king, Behold the head of Ish-bosheth the son of Saul thine enemy, which sought thy life; and the Lord hath avenged my lord the king this day of Saul, and of his seed.
verses.bsb: They brought the head of Ish-bosheth to David at Hebron and said to the king, “Here is the head of Ish-bosheth son of Saul, your enemy who sought your life. Today the LORD has granted vengeance to my lord the king against Saul and his offspring.”

verses.kjv: ¶ And David answered Rechab and Baanah his brother, the sons of Rimmon the Beerothite, and said unto them, As the Lord liveth, who hath redeemed my soul out of all adversity,
verses.bsb: But David answered Rechab and his brother Baanah, the sons of Rimmon the Beerothite, “As surely as the LORD lives, who has redeemed my life from all distress,

verses.kjv: When one told me, saying, Behold, Saul is dead, thinking to have brought good tidings, I took hold of him, and slew him in Ziklag, who thought that I would have given him a reward for his tidings:
verses.bsb: when someone told me, ‘Look, Saul is dead,’ and thought he was a bearer of good news, I seized him and put him to death at Ziklag. That was his reward for his news!

verses.kjv: How much more, when wicked men have slain a righteous person in his own house upon his bed? shall I not therefore now require his blood of your hand, and take you away from the earth?
verses.bsb: How much more, when wicked men kill a righteous man in his own house and on his own bed, shall I not now require his blood from your hands and remove you from the earth!”

verses.kjv: And David commanded his young men, and they slew them, and cut off their hands and their feet, and hanged them up over the pool in Hebron. But they took the head of Ish-bosheth, and buried it in the sepulchre of Abner in Hebron.
verses.bsb: So David commanded his young men, and they killed Rechab and Baanah. They cut off their hands and feet and hung their bodies by the pool in Hebron, but they took the head of Ish-bosheth and buried it in Abner’s tomb in Hebron.

verses.kjv: THEN came all the tribes of Israel to David unto Hebron, and spake, saying, Behold, we are thy bone and thy flesh.
verses.bsb: Then all the tribes of Israel came to David at Hebron and said, “Here we are, your own flesh and blood.

verses.kjv: Also in time past, when Saul was king over us, thou wast he that leddest out and broughtest in Israel: and the Lord said to thee, Thou shalt feed my people Israel, and thou shalt be a captain over Israel.
verses.bsb: Even in times past, while Saul was king over us, you were the one who led Israel out and brought them back. And to you the LORD said, ‘You will shepherd My people Israel, and you will be ruler over them.’”

verses.kjv: So all the elders of Israel came to the king to Hebron; and king David made a league with them in Hebron before the Lord: and they anointed David king over Israel.
verses.bsb: So all the elders of Israel came to the king at Hebron, where King David made with them a covenant before the LORD. And they anointed him king over Israel.

verses.kjv: ¶ David was thirty years old when he began to reign, and he reigned forty years.
verses.bsb: David was thirty years old when he became king, and he reigned forty years.

verses.kjv: In Hebron he reigned over Judah seven years and six months: and in Jerusalem he reigned thirty and three years over all Israel and Judah.
verses.bsb: In Hebron he reigned over Judah seven years and six months, and in Jerusalem he reigned thirty-three years over all Israel and Judah.

verses.kjv: ¶ And the king and his men went to Jerusalem unto the Jebusites, the inhabitants of the land: which spake unto David, saying, Except thou take away the blind and the lame, thou shalt not come in hither: thinking, David cannot come in hither.
verses.bsb: Now the king and his men marched to Jerusalem against the Jebusites who inhabited the land. The Jebusites said to David: “You will never get in here. Even the blind and lame can repel you.” For they thought, “David cannot get in here.”

verses.kjv: Nevertheless David took the strong hold of Zion: the same is the city of David.
verses.bsb: Nevertheless, David captured the fortress of Zion (that is, the City of David).

verses.kjv: And David said on that day, Whosoever getteth up to the gutter, and smiteth the Jebusites, and the lame and the blind, that are hated of David’s soul, he shall be chief and captain. Wherefore they said, The blind and the lame shall not come into the house.
verses.bsb: On that day he said, “Whoever attacks the Jebusites must use the water shaft to reach the lame and blind who are despised by David.” That is why it is said, “The blind and the lame will never enter the palace.”

verses.kjv: So David dwelt in the fort, and called it the city of David. And David built round about from Millo and inward.
verses.bsb: So David took up residence in the fortress and called it the City of David. He built it up all the way around, from the supporting terraces inward.

verses.kjv: And David went on, and grew great, and the Lord God of hosts was with him.
verses.bsb: And David became greater and greater, for the LORD God of Hosts was with him.

verses.kjv: ¶ And Hiram king of Tyre sent messengers to David, and cedar trees, and carpenters, and masons: and they built David an house.
verses.bsb: Now Hiram king of Tyre sent envoys to David, along with cedar logs, carpenters, and stonemasons, and they built a palace for David.

verses.kjv: And David perceived that the Lord had established him king over Israel, and that he had exalted his kingdom for his people Israel’s sake.
verses.bsb: And David realized that the LORD had established him as king over Israel and had exalted his kingdom for the sake of His people Israel.

verses.kjv: ¶ And David took him more concubines and wives out of Jerusalem, after he was come from Hebron: and there were yet sons and daughters born to David.
verses.bsb: After he had arrived from Hebron, David took more concubines and wives from Jerusalem, and more sons and daughters were born to him.

verses.kjv: And these be the names of those that were born unto him in Jerusalem; Shammua, and Shobab, and Nathan, and Solomon,
verses.bsb: These are the names of the children born to him in Jerusalem: Shammua, Shobab, Nathan, Solomon,

verses.kjv: Ibhar also, and Elishua, and Nepheg, and Japhia,
verses.bsb: Ibhar, Elishua, Nepheg, Japhia,

verses.kjv: And Elishama, and Eliada, and Eliphalet.
verses.bsb: Elishama, Eliada, and Eliphelet.

verses.kjv: ¶ But when the Philistines heard that they had anointed David king over Israel, all the Philistines came up to seek David; and David heard of it, and went down to the hold.
verses.bsb: When the Philistines heard that David had been anointed king over Israel, they all went in search of him; but David learned of this and went down to the stronghold.

verses.kjv: The Philistines also came and spread themselves in the valley of Rephaim.
verses.bsb: Now the Philistines had come and spread out in the Valley of Rephaim.

verses.kjv: And David inquired of the Lord, saying, Shall I go up to the Philistines? wilt thou deliver them into mine hand? And the Lord said unto David, Go up: for I will doubtless deliver the Philistines into thine hand.
verses.bsb: So David inquired of the LORD, “Should I go up against the Philistines? Will You deliver them into my hand?”  “Go,” replied the LORD, “for I will surely deliver the Philistines into your hand.”

verses.kjv: And David came to Baal-perazim, and David smote them there, and said, The Lord hath broken forth upon mine enemies before me, as the breach of waters. Therefore he called the name of that place Baal-perazim.
verses.bsb: So David went to Baal-perazim, where he defeated the Philistines and said, “Like a bursting flood, the LORD has burst out against my enemies before me.” So he called that place Baal-perazim.

verses.kjv: And there they left their images, and David and his men burned them.
verses.bsb: There the Philistines abandoned their idols, and David and his men carried them away.

verses.kjv: ¶ And the Philistines came up yet again, and spread themselves in the valley of Rephaim.
verses.bsb: Once again the Philistines came up and spread out in the Valley of Rephaim.

verses.kjv: And when David inquired of the Lord, he said, Thou shalt not go up; but fetch a compass behind them, and come upon them over against the mulberry trees.
verses.bsb: So David inquired of the LORD, who answered, “Do not march straight up, but circle around behind them and attack them in front of the balsam trees.

verses.kjv: And let it be, when thou hearest the sound of a going in the tops of the mulberry trees, that then thou shalt bestir thyself: for then shall the Lord go out before thee, to smite the host of the Philistines.
verses.bsb: As soon as you hear the sound of marching in the tops of the balsam trees, move quickly, because this will mean that the LORD has marched out before you to strike the camp of the Philistines.”

verses.kjv: And David did so, as the Lord had commanded him; and smote the Philistines from Geba until thou come to Gazer.
verses.bsb: So David did as the LORD had commanded him, and he struck down the Philistines all the way from Gibeon to Gezer.

verses.kjv: AGAIN, David gathered together all the chosen men of Israel, thirty thousand.
verses.bsb: David again assembled the chosen men of Israel, thirty thousand in all.

verses.kjv: And David arose, and went with all the people that were with him from Baale of Judah, to bring up from thence the ark of God, whose name is called by the name of the Lord of hosts that dwelleth between the cherubims.
verses.bsb: And he and all his troops set out for Baale of Judah to bring up from there the ark of God, which is called by the Name— the name of the LORD of Hosts, who is enthroned between the cherubim that are on it.

verses.kjv: And they set the ark of God upon a new cart, and brought it out of the house of Abinadab that was in Gibeah: and Uzzah and Ahio, the sons of Abinadab, drave the new cart.
verses.bsb: They set the ark of God on a new cart and brought it from the house of Abinadab, which was on the hill. Uzzah and Ahio, the sons of Abinadab, were guiding the new cart,

verses.kjv: And they brought it out of the house of Abinadab which was at Gibeah, accompanying the ark of God: and Ahio went before the ark.
verses.bsb: bringing with it the ark of God. And Ahio was walking in front of the ark.

verses.kjv: And David and all the house of Israel played before the Lord on all manner of instruments made of fir wood, even on harps, and on psalteries, and on timbrels, and on cornets, and on cymbals.
verses.bsb: David and all the house of Israel were celebrating before the LORD with all kinds of wood instruments, harps, stringed instruments, tambourines, sistrums, and cymbals.

verses.kjv: ¶ And when they came to Nachon’s threshingfloor, Uzzah put forth his hand to the ark of God, and took hold of it; for the oxen shook it.
verses.bsb: When they came to the threshing floor of Nacon, Uzzah reached out and took hold of the ark of God, because the oxen had stumbled.

verses.kjv: And the anger of the Lord was kindled against Uzzah; and God smote him there for his error; and there he died by the ark of God.
verses.bsb: And the anger of the LORD burned against Uzzah, and God struck him down on the spot for his irreverence, and he died there beside the ark of God.

verses.kjv: And David was displeased, because the Lord had made a breach upon Uzzah: and he called the name of the place Perez-uzzah to this day.
verses.bsb: Then David became angry because the LORD had burst forth against Uzzah; so he named that place Perez-uzzah, as it is called to this day.

verses.kjv: And David was afraid of the Lord that day, and said, How shall the ark of the Lord come to me?
verses.bsb: That day David feared the LORD and asked, “How can the ark of the LORD ever come to me?”

verses.kjv: So David would not remove the ark of the Lord unto him into the city of David: but David carried it aside into the house of Obed-edom the Gittite.
verses.bsb: So he was unwilling to move the ark of the LORD to the City of David; instead, he took it aside to the house of Obed-edom the Gittite.

verses.kjv: And the ark of the Lord continued in the house of Obed-edom the Gittite three months: and the Lord blessed Obed-edom, and all his household.
verses.bsb: Thus the ark of the LORD remained in the house of Obed-edom the Gittite for three months, and the LORD blessed him and all his household.

verses.kjv: ¶ And it was told king David, saying, The Lord hath blessed the house of Obed-edom, and all that pertaineth unto him, because of the ark of God. So David went and brought up the ark of God from the house of Obed-edom into the city of David with gladness.
verses.bsb: Now it was reported to King David, “The LORD has blessed the house of Obed-edom and all that belongs to him, because of the ark of God.”  So David went and had the ark of God brought up from the house of Obed-edom into the City of David with rejoicing.

verses.kjv: And it was so, that when they that bare the ark of the Lord had gone six paces, he sacrificed oxen and fatlings.
verses.bsb: When those carrying the ark of the LORD had advanced six paces, he sacrificed an ox and a fattened calf.

verses.kjv: And David danced before the Lord with all his might; and David was girded with a linen ephod.
verses.bsb: And David, wearing a linen ephod, danced with all his might before the LORD,

verses.kjv: So David and all the house of Israel brought up the ark of the Lord with shouting, and with the sound of the trumpet.
verses.bsb: while he and all the house of Israel brought up the ark of the LORD with shouting and the sounding of the ram’s horn.

verses.kjv: And as the ark of the Lord came into the city of David, Michal Saul’s daughter looked through a window, and saw king David leaping and dancing before the Lord; and she despised him in her heart.
verses.bsb: As the ark of the LORD was entering the City of David, Saul’s daughter Michal looked down from a window and saw King David leaping and dancing before the LORD, and she despised him in her heart.

verses.kjv: ¶ And they brought in the ark of the Lord, and set it in his place, in the midst of the tabernacle that David had pitched for it: and David offered burnt offerings and peace offerings before the Lord.
verses.bsb: So they brought the ark of the LORD and set it in its place inside the tent that David had pitched for it. Then David offered burnt offerings and peace offerings before the LORD.

verses.kjv: And as soon as David had made an end of offering burnt offerings and peace offerings, he blessed the people in the name of the Lord of hosts.
verses.bsb: When David had finished sacrificing the burnt offerings and peace offerings, he blessed the people in the name of the LORD of Hosts.

verses.kjv: And he dealt among all the people, even among the whole multitude of Israel, as well to the women as men, to every one a cake of bread, and a good piece of flesh, and a flagon of wine. So all the people departed every one to his house.
verses.bsb: Then he distributed to every man and woman among the multitude of Israel a loaf of bread, a date cake, and a raisin cake. And all the people departed, each for his own home.

verses.kjv: ¶ Then David returned to bless his household. And Michal the daughter of Saul came out to meet David, and said, How glorious was the king of Israel to day, who uncovered himself to day in the eyes of the handmaids of his servants, as one of the vain fellows shamelessly uncovereth himself!
verses.bsb: As soon as David returned home to bless his own household, Saul’s daughter Michal came out to meet him. “How the king of Israel has distinguished himself today!” she said. “He has uncovered himself today in the sight of the maidservants of his subjects, like a vulgar person would do.”

verses.kjv: And David said unto Michal, It was before the Lord, which chose me before thy father, and before all his house, to appoint me ruler over the people of the Lord, over Israel: therefore will I play before the Lord.
verses.bsb: But David said to Michal, “I was dancing before the LORD, who chose me over your father and all his house when He appointed me ruler over the LORD’s people Israel. I will celebrate before the LORD,

verses.kjv: And I will yet be more vile than thus, and will be base in mine own sight: and of the maidservants which thou hast spoken of, of them shall I be had in honour.
verses.bsb: and I will humiliate and humble myself even more than this. Yet I will be honored by the maidservants of whom you have spoken.”

verses.kjv: Therefore Michal the daughter of Saul had no child unto the day of her death.
verses.bsb: And Michal the daughter of Saul had no children to the day of her death.

verses.kjv: AND it came to pass, when the king sat in his house, and the Lord had given him rest round about from all his enemies;
verses.bsb: After the king had settled into his palace and the LORD had given him rest from all his enemies around him,

verses.kjv: That the king said unto Nathan the prophet, See now, I dwell in an house of cedar, but the ark of God dwelleth within curtains.
verses.bsb: he said to Nathan the prophet, “Here I am, living in a house of cedar, while the ark of God remains in a tent.”

verses.kjv: And Nathan said to the king, Go, do all that is in thine heart; for the Lord is with thee.
verses.bsb: And Nathan replied to the king, “Go and do all that is in your heart, for the LORD is with you.”

verses.kjv: ¶ And it came to pass that night, that the word of the Lord came unto Nathan, saying,
verses.bsb: But that night the word of the LORD came to Nathan, saying,

verses.kjv: Go and tell my servant David, Thus saith the Lord, Shalt thou build me an house for me to dwell in?
verses.bsb: “Go and tell My servant David that this is what the LORD says: Are you the one to build for Me a house to dwell in?

verses.kjv: Whereas I have not dwelt in any house since the time that I brought up the children of Israel out of Egypt, even to this day, but have walked in a tent and in a tabernacle.
verses.bsb: For I have not dwelt in a house from the day I brought the Israelites up out of Egypt until this day, but I have moved about with a tent as My dwelling.

verses.kjv: In all the places wherein I have walked with all the children of Israel spake I a word with any of the tribes of Israel, whom I commanded to feed my people Israel, saying, Why build ye not me an house of cedar?
verses.bsb: In all My journeys with all the Israelites, have I ever asked any of the leaders I appointed to shepherd My people Israel, ‘Why haven’t you built Me a house of cedar?’

verses.kjv: Now therefore so shalt thou say unto my servant David, Thus saith the Lord of hosts, I took thee from the sheepcote, from following the sheep, to be ruler over my people, over Israel:
verses.bsb: Now then, you are to tell My servant David that this is what the LORD of Hosts says: I took you from the pasture, from following the flock, to be the ruler over My people Israel.

verses.kjv: And I was with thee whithersoever thou wentest, and have cut off all thine enemies out of thy sight, and have made thee a great name, like unto the name of the great men that are in the earth.
verses.bsb: I have been with you wherever you have gone, and I have cut off all your enemies from before you. Now I will make for you a name like the greatest in the land.

verses.kjv: Moreover I will appoint a place for my people Israel, and will plant them, that they may dwell in a place of their own, and move no more; neither shall the children of wickedness afflict them any more, as beforetime,
verses.bsb: And I will provide a place for My people Israel and will plant them so that they may dwell in a place of their own and be disturbed no more. No longer will the sons of wickedness oppress them as they did at the beginning

verses.kjv: And as since the time that I commanded judges to be over my people Israel, and have caused thee to rest from all thine enemies. Also the Lord telleth thee that he will make thee an house.
verses.bsb: and have done since the day I appointed judges over My people Israel. I will give you rest from all your enemies.  The LORD declares to you that He Himself will establish a house for you.

verses.kjv: ¶ And when thy days be fulfilled, and thou shalt sleep with thy fathers, I will set up thy seed after thee, which shall proceed out of thy bowels, and I will establish his kingdom.
verses.bsb: And when your days are fulfilled and you rest with your fathers, I will raise up your descendant after you, who will come from your own body, and I will establish his kingdom.

verses.kjv: He shall build an house for my name, and I will stablish the throne of his kingdom for ever.
verses.bsb: He will build a house for My Name, and I will establish the throne of his kingdom forever.

verses.kjv: I will be his father, and he shall be my son. If he commit iniquity, I will chasten him with the rod of men, and with the stripes of the children of men:
verses.bsb: I will be his Father, and he will be My son. When he does wrong, I will discipline him with the rod of men and with the blows of the sons of men.

verses.kjv: But my mercy shall not depart away from him, as I took it from Saul, whom I put away before thee.
verses.bsb: But My loving devotion will never be removed from him as I removed it from Saul, whom I moved out of your way.

verses.kjv: And thine house and thy kingdom shall be established for ever before thee: thy throne shall be established for ever.
verses.bsb: Your house and kingdom will endure forever before Me, and your throne will be established forever.”

verses.kjv: According to all these words, and according to all this vision, so did Nathan speak unto David.
verses.bsb: So Nathan relayed to David all the words of this entire vision.

verses.kjv: ¶ Then went king David in, and sat before the Lord, and he said, Who am I, O Lord God? and what is my house, that thou hast brought me hitherto?
verses.bsb: Then King David went in, sat before the LORD, and said, “Who am I, O Lord GOD, and what is my house, that You have brought me this far?

verses.kjv: And this was yet a small thing in thy sight, O Lord God; but thou hast spoken also of thy servant’s house for a great while to come. And is this the manner of man, O Lord God?
verses.bsb: And as if this was a small thing in Your eyes, O Lord GOD, You have also spoken about the future of the house of Your servant. Is this Your custom with man, O Lord GOD?

verses.kjv: And what can David say more unto thee? for thou, Lord God, knowest thy servant.
verses.bsb: What more can David say to You? For You know Your servant, O Lord GOD.

verses.kjv: For thy word’s sake, and according to thine own heart, hast thou done all these great things, to make thy servant know them.
verses.bsb: For the sake of Your word and according to Your own heart, You have accomplished this great thing and revealed it to Your servant.

verses.kjv: Wherefore thou art great, O Lord God: for there is none like thee, neither is there any God beside thee, according to all that we have heard with our ears.
verses.bsb: How great You are, O Lord GOD! For there is none like You, and there is no God but You, according to everything we have heard with our own ears.

verses.kjv: And what one nation in the earth is like thy people, even like Israel, whom God went to redeem for a people to himself, and to make him a name, and to do for you great things and terrible, for thy land, before thy people, which thou redeemedst to thee from Egypt, from the nations and their gods?
verses.bsb: And who is like Your people Israel—the one nation on earth whom God went out to redeem as a people for Himself and to make a name for Himself? You performed great and awesome wonders by driving out nations and their gods from before Your people, whom You redeemed for Yourself from Egypt.

verses.kjv: For thou hast confirmed to thyself thy people Israel to be a people unto thee for ever: and thou, Lord, art become their God.
verses.bsb: For You have established Your people Israel as Your very own forever, and You, O LORD, have become their God.

verses.kjv: And now, O Lord God, the word that thou hast spoken concerning thy servant, and concerning his house, establish it for ever, and do as thou hast said.
verses.bsb: And now, O LORD God, confirm forever the word You have spoken concerning Your servant and his house. Do as You have promised,

verses.kjv: And let thy name be magnified for ever, saying, The Lord of hosts is the God over Israel: and let the house of thy servant David be established before thee.
verses.bsb: so that Your name will be magnified forever when it is said, ‘The LORD of Hosts is God over Israel.’ And the house of Your servant David will be established before You.

verses.kjv: For thou, O Lord of hosts, God of Israel, hast revealed to thy servant, saying, I will build thee an house: therefore hath thy servant found in his heart to pray this prayer unto thee.
verses.bsb: For You, O LORD of Hosts, the God of Israel, have revealed this to Your servant when You said, ‘I will build a house for you.’ Therefore Your servant has found the courage to offer this prayer to You.

verses.kjv: And now, O Lord God, thou art that God, and thy words be true, and thou hast promised this goodness unto thy servant:
verses.bsb: And now, O Lord GOD, You are God! Your words are true, and You have promised this goodness to Your servant.

verses.kjv: Therefore now let it please thee to bless the house of thy servant, that it may continue for ever before thee: for thou, O Lord God, hast spoken it: and with thy blessing let the house of thy servant be blessed for ever.
verses.bsb: Now therefore, may it please You to bless the house of Your servant, that it may continue forever before You. For You, O Lord GOD, have spoken, and with Your blessing the house of Your servant will be blessed forever.”

verses.kjv: AND after this it came to pass, that David smote the Philistines, and subdued them: and David took Metheg-ammah out of the hand of the Philistines.
verses.bsb: Some time later, David defeated the Philistines, subdued them, and took Metheg-ammah from the hand of the Philistines.

verses.kjv: And he smote Moab, and measured them with a line, casting them down to the ground; even with two lines measured he to put to death, and with one full line to keep alive. And so the Moabites became David’s servants, and brought gifts.
verses.bsb: David also defeated the Moabites, made them lie down on the ground, and measured them off with a cord. He measured off with two lengths those to be put to death, and with one length those to be spared. So the Moabites became subject to David and brought him tribute.

verses.kjv: ¶ David smote also Hadadezer, the son of Rehob, king of Zobah, as he went to recover his border at the river Euphrates.
verses.bsb: David also defeated Hadadezer son of Rehob, king of Zobah, who had marched out to restore his dominion along the Euphrates River.

verses.kjv: And David took from him a thousand chariots, and seven hundred horsemen, and twenty thousand footmen: and David houghed all the chariot horses, but reserved of them for an hundred chariots.
verses.bsb: David captured from him a thousand chariots, seven thousand charioteers, and twenty thousand foot soldiers, and he hamstrung all the horses except a hundred he kept for the chariots.

verses.kjv: And when the Syrians of Damascus came to succour Hadadezer king of Zobah, David slew of the Syrians two and twenty thousand men.
verses.bsb: When the Arameans of Damascus came to help King Hadadezer of Zobah, David struck down twenty-two thousand men.

verses.kjv: Then David put garrisons in Syria of Damascus: and the Syrians became servants to David, and brought gifts. And the Lord preserved David whithersoever he went.
verses.bsb: Then he placed garrisons in Aram of Damascus, and the Arameans became subject to David and brought him tribute. So the LORD made David victorious wherever he went.

verses.kjv: And David took the shields of gold that were on the servants of Hadadezer, and brought them to Jerusalem.
verses.bsb: And David took the gold shields that belonged to the officers of Hadadezer and brought them to Jerusalem.

verses.kjv: And from Betah, and from Berothai, cities of Hadadezer, king David took exceeding much brass.
verses.bsb: And from Betah and Berothai, cities of Hadadezer, King David took a large amount of bronze.

verses.kjv: ¶ When Toi king of Hamath heard that David had smitten all the host of Hadadezer,
verses.bsb: When King Toi of Hamath heard that David had defeated the entire army of Hadadezer,

verses.kjv: Then Toi sent Joram his son unto king David, to salute him, and to bless him, because he had fought against Hadadezer, and smitten him: for Hadadezer had wars with Toi. And Joram brought with him vessels of silver, and vessels of gold, and vessels of brass:
verses.bsb: he sent his son Joram to greet King David and bless him for fighting and defeating Hadadezer, who had been at war with Toi. Joram brought with him articles of silver and gold and bronze,

verses.kjv: Which also king David did dedicate unto the Lord, with the silver and gold that he had dedicated of all nations which he subdued;
verses.bsb: and King David dedicated these to the LORD, along with the silver and gold he had dedicated from all the nations he had subdued—

verses.kjv: Of Syria, and of Moab, and of the children of Ammon, and of the Philistines, and of Amalek, and of the spoil of Hadadezer, son of Rehob, king of Zobah.
verses.bsb: from Edom and Moab, from the Ammonites and Philistines and Amalekites, and from the spoil of Hadadezer son of Rehob, king of Zobah.

verses.kjv: And David gat him a name when he returned from smiting of the Syrians in the valley of salt, being eighteen thousand men.
verses.bsb: And David made a name for himself when he returned from striking down eighteen thousand Edomites in the Valley of Salt.

verses.kjv: ¶ And he put garrisons in Edom; throughout all Edom put he garrisons, and all they of Edom became David’s servants. And the Lord preserved David whithersoever he went.
verses.bsb: He placed garrisons throughout Edom, and all the Edomites were subject to David. So the LORD made David victorious wherever he went.

verses.kjv: And David reigned over all Israel; and David executed judgment and justice unto all his people.
verses.bsb: Thus David reigned over all Israel and administered justice and righteousness for all his people:

verses.kjv: And Joab the son of Zeruiah was over the host; and Jehoshaphat the son of Ahilud was recorder;
verses.bsb: Joab son of Zeruiah was over the army;   Jehoshaphat son of Ahilud was the recorder;

verses.kjv: And Zadok the son of Ahitub, and Ahimelech the son of Abiathar, were the priests; and Seraiah was the scribe;
verses.bsb: Zadok son of Ahitub and Ahimelech son of Abiathar were priests;   Seraiah was the scribe;

verses.kjv: And Benaiah the son of Jehoiada was over both the Cherethites and the Pelethites; and David’s sons were chief rulers.
verses.bsb: Benaiah son of Jehoiada was over the Cherethites and Pelethites;   and David’s sons were priestly leaders.

verses.kjv: AND David said, Is there yet any that is left of the house of Saul, that I may shew him kindness for Jonathan’s sake?
verses.bsb: Then David asked, “Is there anyone left from the house of Saul to whom I can show kindness for the sake of Jonathan?”

verses.kjv: And there was of the house of Saul a servant whose name was Ziba. And when they had called him unto David, the king said unto him, Art thou Ziba? And he said, Thy servant is he.
verses.bsb: And there was a servant of Saul’s family named Ziba. They summoned him to David, and the king inquired, “Are you Ziba?”  “I am your servant,” he replied.

verses.kjv: And the king said, Is there not yet any of the house of Saul, that I may shew the kindness of God unto him? And Ziba said unto the king, Jonathan hath yet a son, which is lame on his feet.
verses.bsb: So the king asked, “Is there anyone left of Saul’s family to whom I can show the kindness of God?”  Ziba answered, “There is still Jonathan’s son, who is lame in both feet.”

verses.kjv: And the king said unto him, Where is he? And Ziba said unto the king, Behold, he is in the house of Machir, the son of Ammiel, in Lo-debar.
verses.bsb: “Where is he?” replied the king.  And Ziba said, “Indeed, he is in Lo-debar at the house of Machir son of Ammiel.”

verses.kjv: ¶ Then king David sent, and fetched him out of the house of Machir, the son of Ammiel, from Lo-debar.
verses.bsb: So King David had him brought from the house of Machir son of Ammiel in Lo-debar.

verses.kjv: Now when Mephibosheth, the son of Jonathan, the son of Saul, was come unto David, he fell on his face, and did reverence. And David said, Mephibosheth. And he answered, Behold thy servant!
verses.bsb: And when Mephibosheth son of Jonathan, the son of Saul, came to David, he fell facedown in reverence.  Then David said, “Mephibosheth!”  “I am your servant,” he replied.

verses.kjv: ¶ And David said unto him, Fear not: for I will surely shew thee kindness for Jonathan thy father’s sake, and will restore thee all the land of Saul thy father; and thou shalt eat bread at my table continually.
verses.bsb: “Do not be afraid,” said David, “for surely I will show you kindness for the sake of your father Jonathan. I will restore to you all the land of your grandfather Saul, and you will always eat at my table.”

verses.kjv: And he bowed himself, and said, What is thy servant, that thou shouldest look upon such a dead dog as I am?
verses.bsb: Mephibosheth bowed down and said, “What is your servant, that you should show regard for a dead dog like me?”

verses.kjv: ¶ Then the king called to Ziba, Saul’s servant, and said unto him, I have given unto thy master’s son all that pertained to Saul and to all his house.
verses.bsb: Then the king summoned Saul’s servant Ziba and said to him, “I have given to your master’s grandson all that belonged to Saul and to all his house.

verses.kjv: Thou therefore, and thy sons, and thy servants, shall till the land for him, and thou shalt bring in the fruits, that thy master’s son may have food to eat: but Mephibosheth thy master’s son shall eat bread alway at my table. Now Ziba had fifteen sons and twenty servants.
verses.bsb: You and your sons and servants are to work the ground for him and bring in the harvest, so that your master’s grandson may have food to eat. But Mephibosheth, your master’s grandson, is always to eat at my table.”  Now Ziba had fifteen sons and twenty servants.

verses.kjv: Then said Ziba unto the king, According to all that my lord the king hath commanded his servant, so shall thy servant do. As for Mephibosheth, said the king, he shall eat at my table, as one of the king’s sons.
verses.bsb: And Ziba said to the king, “Your servant will do all that my lord the king has commanded.”  So Mephibosheth ate at David’s table like one of the king’s own sons.

verses.kjv: And Mephibosheth had a young son, whose name was Micha. And all that dwelt in the house of Ziba were servants unto Mephibosheth.
verses.bsb: And Mephibosheth had a young son named Mica, and all who dwelt in the house of Ziba were servants of Mephibosheth.

verses.kjv: So Mephibosheth dwelt in Jerusalem: for he did eat continually at the king’s table; and was lame on both his feet.
verses.bsb: So Mephibosheth lived in Jerusalem, because he always ate at the king’s table, and he was lame in both feet.

verses.kjv: AND it came to pass after this, that the king of the children of Ammon died, and Hanun his son reigned in his stead.
verses.bsb: Some time later, the king of the Ammonites died and was succeeded by his son Hanun.

verses.kjv: Then said David, I will shew kindness unto Hanun the son of Nahash, as his father shewed kindness unto me. And David sent to comfort him by the hand of his servants for his father. And David’s servants came into the land of the children of Ammon.
verses.bsb: And David said, “I will show kindness to Hanun son of Nahash, just as his father showed kindness to me.”  So David sent some of his servants to console Hanun concerning his father. But when they arrived in the land of the Ammonites,

verses.kjv: And the princes of the children of Ammon said unto Hanun their lord, Thinkest thou that David doth honour thy father, that he hath sent comforters unto thee? hath not David rather sent his servants unto thee, to search the city, and to spy it out, and to overthrow it?
verses.bsb: the princes of the Ammonites said to Hanun their lord, “Just because David has sent you comforters, do you really believe he is showing respect for your father? Has not David instead sent his servants to explore the city, spy it out, and overthrow it?”

verses.kjv: Wherefore Hanun took David’s servants, and shaved off the one half of their beards, and cut off their garments in the middle, even to their buttocks, and sent them away.
verses.bsb: So Hanun took David’s servants, shaved off half of each man’s beard, cut off their garments at the hips, and sent them away.

verses.kjv: When they told it unto David, he sent to meet them, because the men were greatly ashamed: and the king said, Tarry at Jericho until your beards be grown, and then return.
verses.bsb: When this was reported to David, he sent messengers to meet the men, since they had been thoroughly humiliated. The king told them, “Stay in Jericho until your beards have grown back, and then return.”

verses.kjv: ¶ And when the children of Ammon saw that they stank before David, the children of Ammon sent and hired the Syrians of Beth-rehob, and the Syrians of Zoba, twenty thousand footmen, and of king Maacah a thousand men, and of Ish-tob twelve thousand men.
verses.bsb: When the Ammonites realized that they had become a stench to David, they hired twenty thousand Aramean foot soldiers from Beth-rehob and Zoba, as well as a thousand men from the king of Maacah and twelve thousand men from Tob.

verses.kjv: And when David heard of it, he sent Joab, and all the host of the mighty men.
verses.bsb: On hearing this, David sent Joab and the entire army of mighty men.

verses.kjv: And the children of Ammon came out, and put the battle in array at the entering in of the gate: and the Syrians of Zoba, and of Rehob, and Ish-tob, and Maacah, were by themselves in the field.
verses.bsb: The Ammonites marched out and arrayed themselves for battle at the entrance of the city gate, while the Arameans of Zobah and Rehob and the men of Tob and Maacah were by themselves in the open country.

verses.kjv: When Joab saw that the front of the battle was against him before and behind, he chose of all the choice men of Israel, and put them in array against the Syrians:
verses.bsb: When Joab saw the battle lines before him and behind him, he selected some of the best men of Israel and arrayed them against the Arameans.

verses.kjv: And the rest of the people he delivered into the hand of Abishai his brother, that he might put them in array against the children of Ammon.
verses.bsb: And he placed the rest of the forces under the command of his brother Abishai, who arrayed them against the Ammonites.

verses.kjv: And he said, If the Syrians be too strong for me, then thou shalt help me: but if the children of Ammon be too strong for thee, then I will come and help thee.
verses.bsb: “If the Arameans are too strong for me,” said Joab, “then you will come to my rescue. And if the Ammonites are too strong for you, then I will come to your rescue.

verses.kjv: Be of good courage, and let us play the men for our people, and for the cities of our God: and the Lord do that which seemeth him good.
verses.bsb: Be strong and let us fight bravely for our people and for the cities of our God. May the LORD do what is good in His sight.”

verses.kjv: And Joab drew nigh, and the people that were with him, unto the battle against the Syrians: and they fled before him.
verses.bsb: So Joab and his troops advanced to fight the Arameans, who fled before him.

verses.kjv: And when the children of Ammon saw that the Syrians were fled, then fled they also before Abishai, and entered into the city. So Joab returned from the children of Ammon, and came to Jerusalem.
verses.bsb: When the Ammonites saw that the Arameans had fled, they too fled before Abishai, and they entered the city. So Joab returned from fighting against the Ammonites and came to Jerusalem.

verses.kjv: ¶ And when the Syrians saw that they were smitten before Israel, they gathered themselves together.
verses.bsb: When the Arameans saw that they had been defeated by Israel, they regrouped.

verses.kjv: And Hadarezer sent, and brought out the Syrians that were beyond the river: and they came to Helam; and Shobach the captain of the host of Hadarezer went before them.
verses.bsb: Hadadezer sent messengers to bring more Arameans from beyond the Euphrates, and they came to Helam with Shobach the commander of Hadadezer’s army leading them.

verses.kjv: And when it was told David, he gathered all Israel together, and passed over Jordan, and came to Helam. And the Syrians set themselves in array against David, and fought with him.
verses.bsb: When this was reported to David, he gathered all Israel, crossed the Jordan, and went to Helam. Then the Arameans arrayed themselves against David and fought against him.

verses.kjv: And the Syrians fled before Israel; and David slew the men of seven hundred chariots of the Syrians, and forty thousand horsemen, and smote Shobach the captain of their host, who died there.
verses.bsb: But the Arameans fled before Israel, and David killed seven hundred charioteers and forty thousand foot soldiers. He also struck down Shobach the commander of their army, who died there.

verses.kjv: And when all the kings that were servants to Hadarezer saw that they were smitten before Israel, they made peace with Israel, and served them. So the Syrians feared to help the children of Ammon any more.
verses.bsb: When all the kings who were subject to Hadadezer saw that they had been defeated by Israel, they made peace with Israel and became subject to them. So the Arameans were afraid to help the Ammonites anymore.

verses.kjv: AND it came to pass, after the year was expired, at the time when kings go forth to battle, that David sent Joab, and his servants with him, and all Israel; and they destroyed the children of Ammon, and besieged Rabbah. But David tarried still at Jerusalem.
verses.bsb: In the spring, at the time when kings march out to war, David sent out Joab and his servants with the whole army of Israel. They destroyed the Ammonites and besieged Rabbah, but David remained in Jerusalem.

verses.kjv: ¶ And it came to pass in an eveningtide, that David arose from off his bed, and walked upon the roof of the king’s house: and from the roof he saw a woman washing herself; and the woman was very beautiful to look upon.
verses.bsb: One evening David got up from his bed and strolled around on the roof of the palace. And from the roof he saw a woman bathing—a very beautiful woman.

verses.kjv: And David sent and inquired after the woman. And one said, Is not this Bath-sheba, the daughter of Eliam, the wife of Uriah the Hittite?
verses.bsb: So David sent and inquired about the woman, and he was told, “This is Bathsheba, the daughter of Eliam and the wife of Uriah the Hittite.”

verses.kjv: And David sent messengers, and took her; and she came in unto him, and he lay with her; for she was purified from her uncleanness: and she returned unto her house.
verses.bsb: Then David sent messengers to get her, and when she came to him, he slept with her. (Now she had just purified herself from her uncleanness.) Then she returned home.

verses.kjv: And the woman conceived, and sent and told David, and said, I am with child.
verses.bsb: And the woman conceived and sent word to David, saying, “I am pregnant.”

verses.kjv: ¶ And David sent to Joab, saying, Send me Uriah the Hittite. And Joab sent Uriah to David.
verses.bsb: At this, David sent orders to Joab: “Send me Uriah the Hittite.” So Joab sent him to David.

verses.kjv: And when Uriah was come unto him, David demanded of him how Joab did, and how the people did, and how the war prospered.
verses.bsb: When Uriah came to him, David asked how Joab and the troops were doing with the war.

verses.kjv: And David said to Uriah, Go down to thy house, and wash thy feet. And Uriah departed out of the king’s house, and there followed him a mess of meat from the king.
verses.bsb: Then he said to Uriah, “Go down to your house and wash your feet.”  So Uriah left the palace, and a gift from the king followed him.

verses.kjv: But Uriah slept at the door of the king’s house with all the servants of his lord, and went not down to his house.
verses.bsb: But Uriah slept at the door of the palace with all his master’s servants; he did not go down to his house.

verses.kjv: And when they had told David, saying, Uriah went not down unto his house, David said unto Uriah, Camest thou not from thy journey? why then didst thou not go down unto thine house?
verses.bsb: And David was told, “Uriah did not go home.”  “Haven’t you just arrived from a journey?” David asked Uriah. “Why didn’t you go home?”

verses.kjv: And Uriah said unto David, The ark, and Israel, and Judah, abide in tents; and my lord Joab, and the servants of my lord, are encamped in the open fields; shall I then go into mine house, to eat and to drink, and to lie with my wife? as thou livest, and as thy soul liveth, I will not do this thing.
verses.bsb: Uriah answered, “The ark and Israel and Judah are dwelling in tents, and my master Joab and his soldiers are camped in the open field. How can I go to my house to eat and drink and sleep with my wife? As surely as you live, and as your soul lives, I will not do such a thing!”

verses.kjv: And David said to Uriah, Tarry here to day also, and to morrow I will let thee depart. So Uriah abode in Jerusalem that day, and the morrow.
verses.bsb: “Stay here one more day,” David said to Uriah, “and tomorrow I will send you back.” So Uriah stayed in Jerusalem that day and the next.

verses.kjv: And when David had called him, he did eat and drink before him; and he made him drunk: and at even he went out to lie on his bed with the servants of his lord, but went not down to his house.
verses.bsb: Then David invited Uriah to eat and drink with him, and he got Uriah drunk. And in the evening Uriah went out to lie down on his cot with his master’s servants, but he did not go home.

verses.kjv: ¶ And it came to pass in the morning, that David wrote a letter to Joab, and sent it by the hand of Uriah.
verses.bsb: The next morning David wrote a letter to Joab and sent it with Uriah.

verses.kjv: And he wrote in the letter, saying, Set ye Uriah in the forefront of the hottest battle, and retire ye from him, that he may be smitten, and die.
verses.bsb: In the letter he wrote: “Put Uriah at the front of the fiercest battle; then withdraw from him, so that he may be struck down and killed.”
