verses.kjv: AND it was told Joab, Behold, the king weepeth and mourneth for Absalom.
verses.bsb: Then it was reported to Joab, “The king is weeping and mourning over Absalom.”

verses.kjv: And the victory that day was turned into mourning unto all the people: for the people heard say that day how the king was grieved for his son.
verses.bsb: And that day’s victory was turned into mourning for all the people, because on that day they were told, “The king is grieving over his son.”

verses.kjv: And the people gat them by stealth that day into the city, as people being ashamed steal away when they flee in battle.
verses.bsb: So they returned to the city quietly that day, as people steal away in humiliation after fleeing a battle.

verses.kjv: But the king covered his face, and the king cried with a loud voice, O my son Absalom, O Absalom, my son, my son!
verses.bsb: But the king covered his face and cried out at the top of his voice, “O my son Absalom! O Absalom, my son, my son!”

verses.kjv: And Joab came into the house to the king, and said, Thou hast shamed this day the faces of all thy servants, which this day have saved thy life, and the lives of thy sons and of thy daughters, and the lives of thy wives, and the lives of thy concubines;
verses.bsb: Then Joab went into the house and said to the king, “Today you have disgraced all your servants who have saved your life and the lives of your sons and daughters, of your wives, and of your concubines.

verses.kjv: In that thou lovest thine enemies, and hatest thy friends. For thou hast declared this day, that thou regardest neither princes nor servants: for this day I perceive, that if Absalom had lived, and all we had died this day, then it had pleased thee well.
verses.bsb: You love those who hate you and hate those who love you! For you have made it clear today that the commanders and soldiers mean nothing to you. I know today that if Absalom were alive and all of us were dead, it would have pleased you!

verses.kjv: Now therefore arise, go forth, and speak comfortably unto thy servants: for I swear by the Lord, if thou go not forth, there will not tarry one with thee this night: and that will be worse unto thee than all the evil that befell thee from thy youth until now.
verses.bsb: Now therefore get up! Go out and speak comfort to your servants, for I swear by the LORD that if you do not go out, not a man will remain with you tonight. This will be worse for you than all the adversity that has befallen you from your youth until now!”

verses.kjv: Then the king arose, and sat in the gate. And they told unto all the people, saying, Behold, the king doth sit in the gate. And all the people came before the king: for Israel had fled every man to his tent.
verses.bsb: So the king got up and sat in the gate, and all the people were told: “Behold, the king is sitting in the gate.” So they all came before the king.  Meanwhile, the Israelites had fled, each man to his home.

verses.kjv: ¶ And all the people were at strife throughout all the tribes of Israel, saying, The king saved us out of the hand of our enemies, and he delivered us out of the hand of the Philistines; and now he is fled out of the land for Absalom.
verses.bsb: And all the people throughout the tribes of Israel were arguing, “The king rescued us from the hand of our enemies and delivered us from the hand of the Philistines, but now he has fled the land because of Absalom.

verses.kjv: And Absalom, whom we anointed over us, is dead in battle. Now therefore why speak ye not a word of bringing the king back?
verses.bsb: But Absalom, the man we anointed over us, has died in battle. So why do you say nothing about restoring the king?”

verses.kjv: ¶ And king David sent to Zadok and to Abiathar the priests, saying, Speak unto the elders of Judah, saying, Why are ye the last to bring the king back to his house? seeing the speech of all Israel is come to the king, even to his house.
verses.bsb: Then King David sent this message to Zadok and Abiathar, the priests: “Say to the elders of Judah, ‘Why should you be the last to restore the king to his palace, since the talk of all Israel has reached the king at his quarters?

verses.kjv: Ye are my brethren, ye are my bones and my flesh: wherefore then are ye the last to bring back the king?
verses.bsb: You are my brothers, my own flesh and blood. So why should you be the last to restore the king?’

verses.kjv: And say ye to Amasa, Art thou not of my bone, and of my flesh? God do so to me, and more also, if thou be not captain of the host before me continually in the room of Joab.
verses.bsb: And say to Amasa, ‘Aren’t you my flesh and blood? May God punish me, and ever so severely, if from this time you are not the commander of my army in place of Joab!’”

verses.kjv: And he bowed the heart of all the men of Judah, even as the heart of one man; so that they sent this word unto the king, Return thou, and all thy servants.
verses.bsb: So he swayed the hearts of all the men of Judah as though they were one man, and they sent word to the king: “Return, you and all your servants.”

verses.kjv: So the king returned, and came to Jordan. And Judah came to Gilgal, to go to meet the king, to conduct the king over Jordan.
verses.bsb: So the king returned, and when he arrived at the Jordan, the men of Judah came to Gilgal to meet him and escort him across the Jordan.

verses.kjv: ¶ And Shimei the son of Gera, a Benjamite, which was of Bahurim, hasted and came down with the men of Judah to meet king David.
verses.bsb: Then Shimei son of Gera, a Benjamite from Bahurim, hurried down with the men of Judah to meet King David,

verses.kjv: And there were a thousand men of Benjamin with him, and Ziba the servant of the house of Saul, and his fifteen sons and his twenty servants with him; and they went over Jordan before the king.
verses.bsb: along with a thousand men of Benjamin, as well as Ziba the steward of the house of Saul and his fifteen sons and twenty servants.  They rushed down to the Jordan before the king

verses.kjv: And there went over a ferry boat to carry over the king’s household, and to do what he thought good. And Shimei the son of Gera fell down before the king, as he was come over Jordan;
verses.bsb: and crossed at the ford to carry over the king’s household and to do what was good in his sight.  When Shimei son of Gera crossed the Jordan, he fell down before the king

verses.kjv: And said unto the king, Let not my lord impute iniquity unto me, neither do thou remember that which thy servant did perversely the day that my lord the king went out of Jerusalem, that the king should take it to his heart.
verses.bsb: and said, “My lord, do not hold me guilty, and do not remember your servant’s wrongdoing on the day my lord the king left Jerusalem. May the king not take it to heart.

verses.kjv: For thy servant doth know that I have sinned: therefore, behold, I am come the first this day of all the house of Joseph to go down to meet my lord the king.
verses.bsb: For your servant knows that I have sinned, so here I am today as the first of all the house of Joseph to come down to meet my lord the king.”

verses.kjv: But Abishai the son of Zeruiah answered and said, Shall not Shimei be put to death for this, because he cursed the Lord’s anointed?
verses.bsb: But Abishai son of Zeruiah said, “Shouldn’t Shimei be put to death for this, because he cursed the LORD’s anointed?”

verses.kjv: And David said, What have I to do with you, ye sons of Zeruiah, that ye should this day be adversaries unto me? shall there any man be put to death this day in Israel? for do not I know that I am this day king over Israel?
verses.bsb: And David replied, “Sons of Zeruiah, what have I to do with you, that you should be my adversaries today? Should any man be put to death in Israel today? Am I not indeed aware that today I am king over Israel?”

verses.kjv: Therefore the king said unto Shimei, Thou shalt not die. And the king sware unto him.
verses.bsb: So the king said to Shimei, “You shall not die.” And the king swore an oath to him.

verses.kjv: ¶ And Mephibosheth the son of Saul came down to meet the king, and had neither dressed his feet, nor trimmed his beard, nor washed his clothes, from the day the king departed until the day he came again in peace.
verses.bsb: Then Mephibosheth, Saul’s grandson, went down to meet the king. He had not cared for his feet or trimmed his mustache or washed his clothes from the day the king had left until the day he returned safely.

verses.kjv: And it came to pass, when he was come to Jerusalem to meet the king, that the king said unto him, Wherefore wentest not thou with me, Mephibosheth?
verses.bsb: And he came from Jerusalem to meet the king, who asked him, “Mephibosheth, why did you not go with me?”

verses.kjv: And he answered, My lord, O king, my servant deceived me: for thy servant said, I will saddle me an ass, that I may ride thereon, and go to the king; because thy servant is lame.
verses.bsb: “My lord the king,” he replied, “because I am lame, I said, ‘I will have my donkey saddled so that I may ride on it and go with the king.’ But my servant Ziba deceived me,

verses.kjv: And he hath slandered thy servant unto my lord the king; but my lord the king is as an angel of God: do therefore what is good in thine eyes.
verses.bsb: and he has slandered your servant to my lord the king.  Yet my lord the king is like the angel of God, so do what is good in your eyes.

verses.kjv: For all of my father’s house were but dead men before my lord the king: yet didst thou set thy servant among them that did eat at thine own table. What right therefore have I yet to cry any more unto the king?
verses.bsb: For all the house of my grandfather deserves death from my lord the king, yet you have set your servant among those who eat at your table. What further right, then, do I have to keep appealing to the king?”

verses.kjv: And the king said unto him, Why speakest thou any more of thy matters? I have said, Thou and Ziba divide the land.
verses.bsb: The king replied, “Why say any more? I hereby declare that you and Ziba are to divide the land.”

verses.kjv: And Mephibosheth said unto the king, Yea, let him take all, forasmuch as my lord the king is come again in peace unto his own house.
verses.bsb: And Mephibosheth said to the king, “Instead, since my lord the king has safely come to his own house, let Ziba take it all!”

verses.kjv: ¶ And Barzillai the Gileadite came down from Rogelim, and went over Jordan with the king, to conduct him over Jordan.
verses.bsb: Now Barzillai the Gileadite had come down from Rogelim to cross the Jordan with the king and send him on his way from there.

verses.kjv: Now Barzillai was a very aged man, even fourscore years old: and he had provided the king of sustenance while he lay at Mahanaim; for he was a very great man.
verses.bsb: Barzillai was quite old, eighty years of age, and since he was a very wealthy man, he had provided for the king while he stayed in Mahanaim.

verses.kjv: And the king said unto Barzillai, Come thou over with me, and I will feed thee with me in Jerusalem.
verses.bsb: The king said to Barzillai, “Cross over with me, and I will provide for you at my side in Jerusalem.”

verses.kjv: And Barzillai said unto the king, How long have I to live, that I should go up with the king unto Jerusalem?
verses.bsb: But Barzillai replied, “How many years of my life remain, that I should go up to Jerusalem with the king?

verses.kjv: I am this day fourscore years old: and can I discern between good and evil? can thy servant taste what I eat or what I drink? can I hear any more the voice of singing men and singing women? wherefore then should thy servant be yet a burden unto my lord the king?
verses.bsb: I am now eighty years old. Can I discern what is good and what is not? Can your servant taste what he eats or drinks? Can I still hear the voice of singing men and women? Why should your servant be an added burden to my lord the king?

verses.kjv: Thy servant will go a little way over Jordan with the king: and why should the king recompense it me with such a reward?
verses.bsb: Your servant could go with the king only a short distance past the Jordan; why should the king repay me with such a reward?

verses.kjv: Let thy servant, I pray thee, turn back again, that I may die in mine own city, and be buried by the grave of my father and of my mother. But behold thy servant Chimham; let him go over with my lord the king; and do to him what shall seem good unto thee.
verses.bsb: Please let your servant return, that I may die in my own city near the tomb of my father and mother. But here is your servant Chimham. Let him cross over with my lord the king, and do for him what is good in your sight.”

verses.kjv: And the king answered, Chimham shall go over with me, and I will do to him that which shall seem good unto thee: and whatsoever thou shalt require of me, that will I do for thee.
verses.bsb: The king replied, “Chimham will cross over with me, and I will do for him what seems good in your sight, and I will do for you whatever you desire of me.”

verses.kjv: And all the people went over Jordan. And when the king was come over, the king kissed Barzillai, and blessed him; and he returned unto his own place.
verses.bsb: So all the people crossed the Jordan, and then the king crossed over. The king kissed Barzillai and blessed him, and Barzillai returned home.

verses.kjv: Then the king went on to Gilgal, and Chimham went on with him: and all the people of Judah conducted the king, and also half the people of Israel.
verses.bsb: Then the king went on to Gilgal, and Chimham crossed over with him. All the troops of Judah and half the troops of Israel escorted the king.

verses.kjv: ¶ And, behold, all the men of Israel came to the king, and said unto the king, Why have our brethren the men of Judah stolen thee away, and have brought the king, and his household, and all David’s men with him, over Jordan?
verses.bsb: Soon all the men of Israel came to the king and asked, “Why did our brothers, the men of Judah, take you away secretly and bring the king and his household across the Jordan, together with all of David’s men?”

verses.kjv: And all the men of Judah answered the men of Israel, Because the king is near of kin to us: wherefore then be ye angry for this matter? have we eaten at all of the king’s cost? or hath he given us any gift?
verses.bsb: And all the men of Judah replied to the men of Israel, “We did this because the king is our relative. Why does this anger you? Have we ever eaten at the king’s expense or received anything for ourselves?”

verses.kjv: And the men of Israel answered the men of Judah, and said, We have ten parts in the king, and we have also more right in David than ye: why then did ye despise us, that our advice should not be first had in bringing back our king? And the words of the men of Judah were fiercer than the words of the men of Israel.
verses.bsb: “We have ten shares in the king,” answered the men of Israel, “so we have more claim to David than you. Why then do you despise us? Were we not the first to speak of restoring our king?”  But the men of Judah pressed even harder than the men of Israel.

verses.kjv: AND there happened to be there a man of Belial, whose name was Sheba, the son of Bichri, a Benjamite: and he blew a trumpet, and said, We have no part in David, neither have we inheritance in the son of Jesse: every man to his tents, O Israel.
verses.bsb: Now a worthless man named Sheba son of Bichri, a Benjamite, happened to be there, and he blew the ram’s horn and shouted:  “We have no share in David, no inheritance in Jesse’s son. Every man to his tent, O Israel!”

verses.kjv: So every man of Israel went up from after David, and followed Sheba the son of Bichri: but the men of Judah clave unto their king, from Jordan even to Jerusalem.
verses.bsb: So all the men of Israel deserted David to follow Sheba son of Bichri. But the men of Judah stayed by their king all the way from the Jordan to Jerusalem.

verses.kjv: ¶ And David came to his house at Jerusalem; and the king took the ten women his concubines, whom he had left to keep the house, and put them in ward, and fed them, but went not in unto them. So they were shut up unto the day of their death, living in widowhood.
verses.bsb: When David returned to his palace in Jerusalem, he took the ten concubines he had left to care for the palace, and he placed them in a house under guard. He provided for them, but he no longer slept with them. They were confined until the day of their death, living as widows.

verses.kjv: ¶ Then said the king to Amasa, Assemble me the men of Judah within three days, and be thou here present.
verses.bsb: Then the king said to Amasa, “Summon the men of Judah to come to me within three days, and be here yourself.”

verses.kjv: So Amasa went to assemble the men of Judah: but he tarried longer than the set time which he had appointed him.
verses.bsb: So Amasa went to summon Judah, but he took longer than the time allotted him.

verses.kjv: And David said to Abishai, Now shall Sheba the son of Bichri do us more harm than did Absalom: take thou thy lord’s servants, and pursue after him, lest he get him fenced cities, and escape us.
verses.bsb: And David said to Abishai, “Now Sheba the son of Bichri will do us more harm than Absalom. Take your lord’s servants and pursue him, or he will find fortified cities and elude us.”

verses.kjv: And there went out after him Joab’s men, and the Cherethites, and the Pelethites, and all the mighty men: and they went out of Jerusalem, to pursue after Sheba the son of Bichri.
verses.bsb: So Joab’s men, along with the Cherethites, the Pelethites, and all the mighty men, marched out of Jerusalem in pursuit of Sheba son of Bichri.

verses.kjv: When they were at the great stone which is in Gibeon, Amasa went before them. And Joab’s garment that he had put on was girded unto him, and upon it a girdle with a sword fastened upon his loins in the sheath thereof; and as he went forth it fell out.
verses.bsb: And while they were at the great stone in Gibeon, Amasa joined them.  Now Joab was dressed in military attire, with a dagger strapped to his belt. And as he stepped forward, he slipped the dagger from its sheath.

verses.kjv: And Joab said to Amasa, Art thou in health, my brother? And Joab took Amasa by the beard with the right hand to kiss him.
verses.bsb: “Are you well, my brother?” Joab asked Amasa. And with his right hand Joab grabbed Amasa by the beard to kiss him.

verses.kjv: But Amasa took no heed to the sword that was in Joab’s hand: so he smote him therewith in the fifth rib, and shed out his bowels to the ground, and struck him not again; and he died. So Joab and Abishai his brother pursued after Sheba the son of Bichri.
verses.bsb: Amasa was not on guard against the dagger in Joab’s hand, and Joab stabbed him in the stomach and spilled out his intestines on the ground. And Joab did not need to strike him again, for Amasa was dead. Then Joab and his brother Abishai pursued Sheba son of Bichri.

verses.kjv: And one of Joab’s men stood by him, and said, He that favoureth Joab, and he that is for David, let him go after Joab.
verses.bsb: One of Joab’s young men stood near Amasa and said, “Whoever favors Joab, and whoever is for David, let him follow Joab!”

verses.kjv: And Amasa wallowed in blood in the midst of the highway. And when the man saw that all the people stood still, he removed Amasa out of the highway into the field, and cast a cloth upon him, when he saw that every one that came by him stood still.
verses.bsb: But Amasa wallowed in his blood in the middle of the road, and when the man saw that all the troops were stopping there, he dragged the body off the road into a field and threw a garment over it.

verses.kjv: When he was removed out of the highway, all the people went on after Joab, to pursue after Sheba the son of Bichri.
verses.bsb: As soon as Amasa’s body was removed from the road, all the men went on with Joab to pursue Sheba son of Bichri.

verses.kjv: ¶ And he went through all the tribes of Israel unto Abel, and to Beth-maachah, and all the Berites: and they were gathered together, and went also after him.
verses.bsb: Sheba passed through all the tribes of Israel to Abel-beth-maacah and through the entire region of the Berites, who gathered together and followed him.

verses.kjv: And they came and besieged him in Abel of Beth-maachah, and they cast up a bank against the city, and it stood in the trench: and all the people that were with Joab battered the wall, to throw it down.
verses.bsb: And Joab’s troops came and besieged Sheba in Abel-beth-maacah and built a siege ramp against the outer rampart of the city.  As all the troops with Joab were battering the wall to topple it,

verses.kjv: ¶ Then cried a wise woman out of the city, Hear, hear; say, I pray you, unto Joab, Come near hither, that I may speak with thee.
verses.bsb: a wise woman called out from the city, “Listen! Listen! Please tell Joab to come here so that I may speak with him.”

verses.kjv: And when he was come near unto her, the woman said, Art thou Joab? And he answered, I am he. Then she said unto him, Hear the words of thine handmaid. And he answered, I do hear.
verses.bsb: When he had come near to her, the woman asked, “Are you Joab?”  “I am,” he replied.  “Listen to the words of your servant,” she said.  “I am listening,” he answered.

verses.kjv: Then she spake, saying, They were wont to speak in old time, saying, They shall surely ask counsel at Abel: and so they ended the matter.
verses.bsb: Then the woman said, “Long ago they used to say, ‘Seek counsel at Abel,’ and that is how disputes were settled.

verses.kjv: I am one of them that are peaceable and faithful in Israel: thou seekest to destroy a city and a mother in Israel: why wilt thou swallow up the inheritance of the Lord?
verses.bsb: I am among the peaceable and faithful in Israel, but you are trying to destroy a city that is a mother in Israel. Why would you swallow up the LORD’s inheritance?”

verses.kjv: And Joab answered and said, Far be it, far be it from me, that I should swallow up or destroy.
verses.bsb: “Far be it!” Joab declared. “Far be it from me to swallow up or destroy!

verses.kjv: The matter is not so: but a man of mount Ephraim, Sheba the son of Bichri by name, hath lifted up his hand against the king, even against David: deliver him only, and I will depart from the city. And the woman said unto Joab, Behold, his head shall be thrown to thee over the wall.
verses.bsb: That is not the case. But a man named Sheba son of Bichri, from the hill country of Ephraim, has lifted up his hand against the king, against David. Deliver him alone, and I will depart from the city.”  “Look,” the woman replied, “his head will be thrown to you over the wall.”

verses.kjv: Then the woman went unto all the people in her wisdom. And they cut off the head of Sheba the son of Bichri, and cast it out to Joab. And he blew a trumpet, and they retired from the city, every man to his tent. And Joab returned to Jerusalem unto the king.
verses.bsb: Then the woman went to all the people with her wise counsel, and they cut off the head of Sheba son of Bichri and threw it to Joab. So he blew the ram’s horn and his men dispersed from the city, each to his own home. And Joab returned to the king in Jerusalem.

verses.kjv: ¶ Now Joab was over all the host of Israel: and Benaiah the son of Jehoiada was over the Cherethites and over the Pelethites:
verses.bsb: Now Joab was over the whole army of Israel; Benaiah son of Jehoiada was over the Cherethites and Pelethites;

verses.kjv: And Adoram was over the tribute: and Jehoshaphat the son of Ahilud was recorder:
verses.bsb: Adoram was in charge of the forced labor; Jehoshaphat son of Ahilud was the recorder;

verses.kjv: And Sheva was scribe: and Zadok and Abiathar were the priests:
verses.bsb: Sheva was the scribe; Zadok and Abiathar were priests;

verses.kjv: And Ira also the Jairite was a chief ruler about David.
verses.bsb: and Ira the Jairite was David’s priest.

verses.kjv: THEN there was a famine in the days of David three years, year after year; and David inquired of the Lord. And the Lord answered, It is for Saul, and for his bloody house, because he slew the Gibeonites.
verses.bsb: During the reign of David there was a famine for three successive years, and David sought the face of the LORD.  And the LORD said, “It is because of the blood shed by Saul and his family, because he killed the Gibeonites.”

verses.kjv: And the king called the Gibeonites, and said unto them; (now the Gibeonites were not of the children of Israel, but of the remnant of the Amorites; and the children of Israel had sworn unto them: and Saul sought to slay them in his zeal to the children of Israel and Judah.)
verses.bsb: At this, David summoned the Gibeonites and spoke to them. (Now the Gibeonites were not Israelites, but a remnant of the Amorites. The Israelites had taken an oath concerning them, but in his zeal for Israel and Judah, Saul had sought to kill them.)

verses.kjv: Wherefore David said unto the Gibeonites, What shall I do for you? and wherewith shall I make the atonement, that ye may bless the inheritance of the Lord?
verses.bsb: So David asked the Gibeonites, “What shall I do for you? How can I make amends so that you may bless the inheritance of the LORD?”

verses.kjv: And the Gibeonites said unto him, We will have no silver nor gold of Saul, nor of his house; neither for us shalt thou kill any man in Israel. And he said, What ye shall say, that will I do for you.
verses.bsb: The Gibeonites said to him, “We need no silver or gold from Saul or his house, nor should you put to death anyone in Israel for us.”  “Whatever you ask, I will do for you,” he replied.

verses.kjv: And they answered the king, The man that consumed us, and that devised against us that we should be destroyed from remaining in any of the coasts of Israel,
verses.bsb: And they answered the king, “As for the man who consumed us and plotted against us to exterminate us from existing within any border of Israel,

verses.kjv: Let seven men of his sons be delivered unto us, and we will hang them up unto the Lord in Gibeah of Saul, whom the Lord did choose. And the king said, I will give them.
verses.bsb: let seven of his male descendants be delivered to us so that we may hang them before the LORD at Gibeah of Saul, the chosen of the LORD.”  “I will give them to you,” said the king.

verses.kjv: But the king spared Mephibosheth, the son of Jonathan the son of Saul, because of the Lord’s oath that was between them, between David and Jonathan the son of Saul.
verses.bsb: Now the king spared Mephibosheth son of Jonathan, the son of Saul, because of the oath before the LORD between David and Jonathan son of Saul.

verses.kjv: But the king took the two sons of Rizpah the daughter of Aiah, whom she bare unto Saul, Armoni and Mephibosheth; and the five sons of Michal the daughter of Saul, whom she brought up for Adriel the son of Barzillai the Meholathite:
verses.bsb: But the king took Armoni and Mephibosheth, the two sons whom Rizpah daughter of Aiah had borne to Saul, as well as the five sons whom Merab daughter of Saul had borne to Adriel son of Barzillai the Meholathite.

verses.kjv: And he delivered them into the hands of the Gibeonites, and they hanged them in the hill before the Lord: and they fell all seven together, and were put to death in the days of harvest, in the first days, in the beginning of barley harvest.
verses.bsb: And he delivered them into the hands of the Gibeonites, and they hanged them on the hill before the LORD. So all seven of them fell together; they were put to death in the first days of the harvest, at the beginning of the barley harvest.

verses.kjv: ¶ And Rizpah the daughter of Aiah took sackcloth, and spread it for her upon the rock, from the beginning of harvest until water dropped upon them out of heaven, and suffered neither the birds of the air to rest on them by day, nor the beasts of the field by night.
verses.bsb: And Rizpah the daughter of Aiah took sackcloth and spread it out for herself on a rock. From the beginning of the harvest until the rain from heaven poured down on the bodies, she did not allow the birds of the air to rest on them by day, nor the beasts of the field by night.

verses.kjv: And it was told David what Rizpah the daughter of Aiah, the concubine of Saul, had done.
verses.bsb: When David was told what Saul’s concubine Rizpah, daughter of Aiah, had done,

verses.kjv: ¶ And David went and took the bones of Saul and the bones of Jonathan his son from the men of Jabesh-gilead, which had stolen them from the street of Beth-shan, where the Philistines had hanged them, when the Philistines had slain Saul in Gilboa:
verses.bsb: he went and took the bones of Saul and his son Jonathan from the men of Jabesh-gilead, who had stolen them from the public square of Beth-shan where the Philistines had hung the bodies after they had struck down Saul at Gilboa.

verses.kjv: And he brought up from thence the bones of Saul and the bones of Jonathan his son; and they gathered the bones of them that were hanged.
verses.bsb: So David had the bones of Saul and his son Jonathan brought from there, along with the bones of those who had been hanged.

verses.kjv: And the bones of Saul and Jonathan his son buried they in the country of Benjamin in Zelah, in the sepulchre of Kish his father: and they performed all that the king commanded. And after that God was intreated for the land.
verses.bsb: And they buried the bones of Saul and his son Jonathan in Zela in the land of Benjamin, in the tomb of Saul’s father Kish.  After they had done everything the king had commanded, God answered their prayers for the land.

verses.kjv: ¶ Moreover the Philistines had yet war again with Israel; and David went down, and his servants with him, and fought against the Philistines: and David waxed faint.
verses.bsb: Once again the Philistines waged war against Israel, and David and his servants went down and fought against the Philistines; but David became exhausted.

verses.kjv: And Ishbi-benob, which was of the sons of the giant, the weight of whose spear weighed three hundred shekels of brass in weight, he being girded with a new sword, thought to have slain David.
verses.bsb: Then Ishbi-benob, a descendant of Rapha, whose bronze spear weighed three hundred shekels and who was bearing a new sword, resolved to kill David.

verses.kjv: But Abishai the son of Zeruiah succoured him, and smote the Philistine, and killed him. Then the men of David sware unto him, saying, Thou shalt go no more out with us to battle, that thou quench not the light of Israel.
verses.bsb: But Abishai son of Zeruiah came to his aid, struck the Philistine, and killed him.  Then David’s men swore to him, “You must never again go out with us to battle, so that the lamp of Israel may not be extinguished.”

verses.kjv: And it came to pass after this, that there was again a battle with the Philistines at Gob: then Sibbechai the Hushathite slew Saph, which was of the sons of the giant.
verses.bsb: Some time later at Gob, there was another battle with the Philistines. At that time Sibbecai the Hushathite killed Saph, one of the descendants of Rapha.

verses.kjv: And there was again a battle in Gob with the Philistines, where Elhanan the son of Jaare-oregim, a Beth-lehemite, slew the brother of Goliath the Gittite, the staff of whose spear was like a weaver’s beam.
verses.bsb: Once again there was a battle with the Philistines at Gob, and Elhanan son of Jair the Bethlehemite killed the brother of Goliath the Gittite, the shaft of whose spear was like a weaver’s beam.

verses.kjv: And there was yet a battle in Gath, where was a man of great stature, that had on every hand six fingers, and on every foot six toes, four and twenty in number; and he also was born to the giant.
verses.bsb: And there was still another battle at Gath, where there was a man of great stature with six fingers on each hand and six toes on each foot—twenty-four in all. He too was descended from Rapha,

verses.kjv: And when he defied Israel, Jonathan the son of Shimea the brother of David slew him.
verses.bsb: and when he taunted Israel, Jonathan the son of David’s brother Shimei killed him.

verses.kjv: These four were born to the giant in Gath, and fell by the hand of David, and by the hand of his servants.
verses.bsb: So these four descendants of Rapha in Gath fell at the hands of David and his servants.

verses.kjv: AND David spake unto the Lord the words of this song in the day that the Lord had delivered him out of the hand of all his enemies, and out of the hand of Saul:
verses.bsb: And David sang this song to the LORD on the day the LORD had delivered him from the hand of all his enemies and from the hand of Saul.

verses.kjv: And he said, The Lord is my rock, and my fortress, and my deliverer;
verses.bsb: He said:  “The LORD is my rock, my fortress, and my deliverer.

verses.kjv: The God of my rock; in him will I trust: he is my shield, and the horn of my salvation, my high tower, and my refuge, my saviour; thou savest me from violence.
verses.bsb: My God is my rock, in whom I take refuge, my shield, and the horn of my salvation. My stronghold, my refuge, and my Savior, You save me from violence.

verses.kjv: I will call on the Lord, who is worthy to be praised: so shall I be saved from mine enemies.
verses.bsb: I will call upon the LORD, who is worthy to be praised; so shall I be saved from my enemies.

verses.kjv: When the waves of death compassed me, the floods of ungodly men made me afraid;
verses.bsb: For the waves of death engulfed me; the torrents of chaos overwhelmed me.

verses.kjv: The sorrows of hell compassed me about; the snares of death prevented me;
verses.bsb: The cords of Sheol entangled me; the snares of death confronted me.

verses.kjv: In my distress I called upon the Lord, and cried to my God: and he did hear my voice out of his temple, and my cry did enter into his ears.
verses.bsb: In my distress I called upon the LORD; I cried out to my God. And from His temple He heard my voice, and my cry for help reached His ears.

verses.kjv: Then the earth shook and trembled; the foundations of heaven moved and shook, because he was wroth.
verses.bsb: Then the earth shook and quaked; the foundations of the heavens trembled; they were shaken because He burned with anger.

verses.kjv: There went up a smoke out of his nostrils, and fire out of his mouth devoured: coals were kindled by it.
verses.bsb: Smoke rose from His nostrils, and consuming fire came from His mouth; glowing coals blazed forth.

verses.kjv: He bowed the heavens also, and came down; and darkness was under his feet.
verses.bsb: He parted the heavens and came down with dark clouds beneath His feet.

verses.kjv: And he rode upon a cherub, and did fly: and he was seen upon the wings of the wind.
verses.bsb: He mounted a cherub and flew; He soared on the wings of the wind.

verses.kjv: And he made darkness pavilions round about him, dark waters, and thick clouds of the skies.
verses.bsb: He made darkness a canopy around Him, a gathering of water and thick clouds.

verses.kjv: Through the brightness before him were coals of fire kindled.
verses.bsb: From the brightness of His presence coals of fire blazed forth.

verses.kjv: The Lord thundered from heaven, and the most High uttered his voice.
verses.bsb: The LORD thundered from heaven; the voice of the Most High resounded.

verses.kjv: And he sent out arrows, and scattered them; lightning, and discomfited them.
verses.bsb: He shot His arrows and scattered the foes; He hurled lightning and routed them.

verses.kjv: And the channels of the sea appeared, the foundations of the world were discovered, at the rebuking of the Lord, at the blast of the breath of his nostrils.
verses.bsb: The channels of the sea appeared, and the foundations of the world were exposed at the rebuke of the LORD, at the blast of the breath of His nostrils.

verses.kjv: He sent from above, he took me; he drew me out of many waters;
verses.bsb: He reached down from on high and took hold of me; He drew me out of deep waters.

verses.kjv: He delivered me from my strong enemy, and from them that hated me: for they were too strong for me.
verses.bsb: He rescued me from my powerful enemy, from foes too mighty for me.

verses.kjv: They prevented me in the day of my calamity: but the Lord was my stay.
verses.bsb: They confronted me in my day of calamity, but the LORD was my support.

verses.kjv: He brought me forth also into a large place: he delivered me, because he delighted in me.
verses.bsb: He brought me out into the open; He rescued me because He delighted in me.

verses.kjv: The Lord rewarded me according to my righteousness: according to the cleanness of my hands hath he recompensed me.
verses.bsb: The LORD has rewarded me according to my righteousness; He has repaid me according to the cleanness of my hands.

verses.kjv: For I have kept the ways of the Lord, and have not wickedly departed from my God.
verses.bsb: For I have kept the ways of the LORD and have not wickedly departed from my God.

verses.kjv: For all his judgments were before me: and as for his statutes, I did not depart from them.
verses.bsb: For all His ordinances are before me; I have not disregarded His statutes.

verses.kjv: I was also upright before him, and have kept myself from mine iniquity.
verses.bsb: And I have been blameless before Him and kept myself from iniquity.

verses.kjv: Therefore the Lord hath recompensed me according to my righteousness; according to my cleanness in his eye sight.
verses.bsb: So the LORD has repaid me according to my righteousness, according to my cleanness in His sight.

verses.kjv: With the merciful thou wilt shew thyself merciful, and with the upright man thou wilt shew thyself upright.
verses.bsb: To the faithful You show Yourself faithful, to the blameless You show Yourself blameless;

verses.kjv: With the pure thou wilt shew thyself pure; and with the froward thou wilt shew thyself unsavoury.
verses.bsb: to the pure You show Yourself pure, but to the crooked You show Yourself shrewd.

verses.kjv: And the afflicted people thou wilt save: but thine eyes are upon the haughty, that thou mayest bring them down.
verses.bsb: You save an afflicted people, but Your eyes are on the haughty to bring them down.

verses.kjv: For thou art my lamp, O Lord: and the Lord will lighten my darkness.
verses.bsb: For You, O LORD, are my lamp; the LORD lights up my darkness.

verses.kjv: For by thee I have run through a troop: by my God have I leaped over a wall.
verses.bsb: For in You I can charge an army; with my God I can scale a wall.

verses.kjv: As for God, his way is perfect; the word of the Lord is tried: he is a buckler to all them that trust in him.
verses.bsb: As for God, His way is perfect; the word of the LORD is flawless. He is a shield to all who take refuge in Him.

verses.kjv: For who is God, save the Lord? and who is a rock, save our God?
verses.bsb: For who is God besides the LORD? And who is the Rock except our God?

verses.kjv: God is my strength and power: and he maketh my way perfect.
verses.bsb: God is my strong fortress and He makes my way clear.

verses.kjv: He maketh my feet like hinds’ feet: and setteth me upon my high places.
verses.bsb: He makes my feet like those of a deer and stations me upon the heights.

verses.kjv: He teacheth my hands to war; so that a bow of steel is broken by mine arms.
verses.bsb: He trains my hands for battle; my arms can bend a bow of bronze.

verses.kjv: Thou hast also given me the shield of thy salvation: and thy gentleness hath made me great.
verses.bsb: You have given me Your shield of salvation, and Your gentleness exalts me.

verses.kjv: Thou hast enlarged my steps under me; so that my feet did not slip.
verses.bsb: You broaden the path beneath me so that my ankles do not give way.

verses.kjv: I have pursued mine enemies, and destroyed them; and turned not again until I had consumed them.
verses.bsb: I pursued my enemies and destroyed them; I did not turn back until they were consumed.

verses.kjv: And I have consumed them, and wounded them, that they could not arise: yea, they are fallen under my feet.
verses.bsb: I devoured and crushed them so they could not rise; they have fallen under my feet.

verses.kjv: For thou hast girded me with strength to battle: them that rose up against me hast thou subdued under me.
verses.bsb: You have armed me with strength for battle; You have subdued my foes beneath me.

verses.kjv: Thou hast also given me the necks of mine enemies, that I might destroy them that hate me.
verses.bsb: You have made my enemies retreat before me; I put an end to those who hated me.

verses.kjv: They looked, but there was none to save; even unto the Lord, but he answered them not.
verses.bsb: They looked, but there was no one to save them— to the LORD, but He did not answer.

verses.kjv: Then did I beat them as small as the dust of the earth, I did stamp them as the mire of the street, and did spread them abroad.
verses.bsb: I ground them as the dust of the earth; I crushed and trampled them like mud in the streets.

verses.kjv: Thou also hast delivered me from the strivings of my people, thou hast kept me to be head of the heathen: a people which I knew not shall serve me.
verses.bsb: You have delivered me from the strife of my people; You have preserved me as the head of nations; a people I had not known shall serve me.

verses.kjv: Strangers shall submit themselves unto me: as soon as they hear, they shall be obedient unto me.
verses.bsb: Foreigners cower before me; when they hear me, they obey me.

verses.kjv: Strangers shall fade away, and they shall be afraid out of their close places.
verses.bsb: Foreigners lose heart and come trembling from their strongholds.

verses.kjv: The Lord liveth; and blessed be my rock; and exalted be the God of the rock of my salvation.
verses.bsb: The LORD lives, and blessed be my Rock! And may God, the Rock of my salvation, be exalted—

verses.kjv: It is God that avengeth me, and that bringeth down the people under me,
verses.bsb: the God who avenges me and brings down nations beneath me,

verses.kjv: And that bringeth me forth from mine enemies: thou also hast lifted me up on high above them that rose up against me: thou hast delivered me from the violent man.
verses.bsb: who frees me from my enemies. You exalt me above my foes; You rescue me from violent men.

verses.kjv: Therefore I will give thanks unto thee, O Lord, among the heathen, and I will sing praises unto thy name.
verses.bsb: Therefore I will praise You, O LORD, among the nations; I will sing praises to Your name.

verses.kjv: He is the tower of salvation for his king: and sheweth mercy to his anointed, unto David, and to his seed for evermore.
verses.bsb: Great salvation He brings to His king. He shows loving devotion to His anointed, to David and his descendants forever.”

verses.kjv: NOW these be the last words of David. David the son of Jesse said, and the man who was raised up on high, the anointed of the God of Jacob, and the sweet psalmist of Israel, said,
verses.bsb: These are the last words of David:  “The oracle of David son of Jesse, the oracle of the man raised on high, the one anointed by the God of Jacob, and the sweet psalmist of Israel:

verses.kjv: The Spirit of the Lord spake by me, and his word was in my tongue.
verses.bsb: The Spirit of the LORD spoke through me; His word was on my tongue.

verses.kjv: The God of Israel said, the Rock of Israel spake to me, He that ruleth over men must be just, ruling in the fear of God.
verses.bsb: The God of Israel spoke; the Rock of Israel said to me, ‘He who rules the people with justice, who rules in the fear of God,

verses.kjv: And he shall be as the light of the morning, when the sun riseth, even a morning without clouds; as the tender grass springing out of the earth by clear shining after rain.
verses.bsb: is like the light of the morning at sunrise of a cloudless dawn, the glistening after the rain on the sprouting grass of the earth.’

verses.kjv: Although my house be not so with God; yet he hath made with me an everlasting covenant, ordered in all things, and sure: for this is all my salvation, and all my desire, although he make it not to grow.
verses.bsb: Is not my house right with God? For He has established with me an everlasting covenant, ordered and secured in every part. Will He not bring about my full salvation and my every desire?

verses.kjv: ¶ But the sons of Belial shall be all of them as thorns thrust away, because they cannot be taken with hands:
verses.bsb: But the worthless are all like thorns raked aside, for they can never be gathered by hand.

verses.kjv: But the man that shall touch them must be fenced with iron and the staff of a spear; and they shall be utterly burned with fire in the same place.
verses.bsb: The man who touches them must be armed with iron or with the shaft of a spear. The fire burns them to ashes in the place where they lie.”

verses.kjv: ¶ These be the names of the mighty men whom David had: The Tachmonite that sat in the seat, chief among the captains; the same was Adino the Eznite: he lift up his spear against eight hundred, whom he slew at one time.
verses.bsb: These are the names of David’s mighty men:  Josheb-basshebeth the Tahchemonite was chief of the Three. He wielded his spear against eight hundred men, whom he killed at one time.

verses.kjv: And after him was Eleazar the son of Dodo the Ahohite, one of the three mighty men with David, when they defied the Philistines that were there gathered together to battle, and the men of Israel were gone away:
verses.bsb: Next in command was Eleazar son of Dodo the Ahohite. As one of the three mighty men, he went with David to taunt the Philistines who had gathered for battle at Pas-dammim. The men of Israel retreated,

verses.kjv: He arose, and smote the Philistines until his hand was weary, and his hand clave unto the sword: and the Lord wrought a great victory that day; and the people returned after him only to spoil.
verses.bsb: but Eleazar stood his ground and struck the Philistines until his hand grew weary and stuck to his sword. The LORD brought about a great victory that day. Then the troops returned to him, but only to plunder the dead.

verses.kjv: And after him was Shammah the son of Agee the Hararite. And the Philistines were gathered together into a troop, where was a piece of ground full of lentiles: and the people fled from the Philistines.
verses.bsb: And after him was Shammah son of Agee the Hararite. When the Philistines had banded together near a field full of lentils, Israel’s troops fled from them.

verses.kjv: But he stood in the midst of the ground, and defended it, and slew the Philistines: and the Lord wrought a great victory.
verses.bsb: But Shammah took his stand in the middle of the field, defended it, and struck down the Philistines. So the LORD brought about a great victory.

verses.kjv: And three of the thirty chief went down, and came to David in the harvest time unto the cave of Adullam: and the troop of the Philistines pitched in the valley of Rephaim.
verses.bsb: At harvest time, three of the thirty chief men went down to David at the cave of Adullam, while a company of Philistines was encamped in the Valley of Rephaim.

verses.kjv: And David was then in an hold, and the garrison of the Philistines was then in Beth-lehem.
verses.bsb: At that time David was in the stronghold, and the garrison of the Philistines was at Bethlehem.

verses.kjv: And David longed, and said, Oh that one would give me drink of the water of the well of Beth-lehem, which is by the gate!
verses.bsb: David longed for water and said, “Oh, that someone would get me a drink of water from the well near the gate of Bethlehem!”

verses.kjv: And the three mighty men brake through the host of the Philistines, and drew water out of the well of Beth-lehem, that was by the gate, and took it, and brought it to David: nevertheless he would not drink thereof, but poured it out unto the Lord.
verses.bsb: So the three mighty men broke through the Philistine camp, drew water from the well near the gate of Bethlehem, and brought it back to David. But he refused to drink it; instead, he poured it out to the LORD,

verses.kjv: And he said, Be it far from me, O Lord, that I should do this: is not this the blood of the men that went in jeopardy of their lives? therefore he would not drink it. These things did these three mighty men.
verses.bsb: saying, “Far be it from me, O LORD, to do this! Is this not the blood of the men who risked their lives?” So he refused to drink it.  Such were the exploits of the three mighty men.

verses.kjv: And Abishai, the brother of Joab, the son of Zeruiah, was chief among three. And he lifted up his spear against three hundred, and slew them, and had the name among three.
verses.bsb: Now Abishai, the brother of Joab and son of Zeruiah, was chief of the Three, and he lifted his spear against three hundred men, killed them, and won a name along with the Three.

verses.kjv: Was he not most honourable of three? therefore he was their captain: howbeit he attained not unto the first three.
verses.bsb: Was he not more honored than the Three? And he became their commander, even though he was not included among the Three.

verses.kjv: And Benaiah the son of Jehoiada, the son of a valiant man, of Kabzeel, who had done many acts, he slew two lionlike men of Moab: he went down also and slew a lion in the midst of a pit in time of snow:
verses.bsb: And Benaiah son of Jehoiada was a man of valor from Kabzeel, a man of many exploits. He struck down two champions of Moab, and on a snowy day he went down into a pit and killed a lion.

verses.kjv: And he slew an Egyptian, a goodly man: and the Egyptian had a spear in his hand; but he went down to him with a staff, and plucked the spear out of the Egyptian’s hand, and slew him with his own spear.
verses.bsb: He also killed an Egyptian, a huge man. Although the Egyptian had a spear in his hand, Benaiah went against him with a club, snatched the spear from his hand, and killed the Egyptian with his own spear.

verses.kjv: These things did Benaiah the son of Jehoiada, and had the name among three mighty men.
verses.bsb: These were the exploits of Benaiah son of Jehoiada, who won a name alongside the three mighty men.

verses.kjv: He was more honourable than the thirty, but he attained not to the first three. And David set him over his guard.
verses.bsb: He was most honored among the Thirty, but he did not become one of the Three. And David appointed him over his guard.

verses.kjv: Asahel the brother of Joab was one of the thirty; Elhanan the son of Dodo of Beth-lehem,
verses.bsb: Now these were members of the Thirty:  Asahel the brother of Joab,   Elhanan son of Dodo of Bethlehem,

verses.kjv: Shammah the Harodite, Elika the Harodite,
verses.bsb: Shammah the Harodite,   Elika the Harodite,

verses.kjv: Helez the Paltite, Ira the son of Ikkesh the Tekoite,
verses.bsb: Helez the Paltite,   Ira son of Ikkesh the Tekoite,

verses.kjv: Abiezer the Anethothite, Mebunnai the Hushathite,
verses.bsb: Abiezer the Anathothite,   Mebunnai the Hushathite,

verses.kjv: Zalmon the Ahohite, Maharai the Netophathite,
verses.bsb: Zalmon the Ahohite,   Maharai the Netophathite,

verses.kjv: Heleb the son of Baanah, a Netophathite, Ittai the son of Ribai out of Gibeah of the children of Benjamin,
verses.bsb: Heled son of Baanah the Netophathite,   Ittai son of Ribai from Gibeah of the Benjamites,

verses.kjv: Benaiah the Pirathonite, Hiddai of the brooks of Gaash,
verses.bsb: Benaiah the Pirathonite,   Hiddai from the brooks of Gaash,

verses.kjv: Abi-albon the Arbathite, Azmaveth the Barhumite,
verses.bsb: Abi-albon the Arbathite,   Azmaveth the Barhumite,

verses.kjv: Eliahba the Shaalbonite, of the sons of Jashen, Jonathan,
verses.bsb: Eliahba the Shaalbonite,   the sons of Jashen,

verses.kjv: Shammah the Hararite, Ahiam the son of Sharar the Hararite,
verses.bsb: Jonathan son of Shammah the Hararite,   Ahiam son of Sharar the Hararite,

verses.kjv: Eliphelet the son of Ahasbai, the son of the Maachathite, Eliam the son of Ahithophel the Gilonite,
verses.bsb: Eliphelet son of Ahasbai the Maacathite,   Eliam son of Ahithophel the Gilonite,

verses.kjv: Hezrai the Carmelite, Paarai the Arbite,
verses.bsb: Hezro the Carmelite,   Paarai the Arbite,

verses.kjv: Igal the son of Nathan of Zobah, Bani the Gadite,
verses.bsb: Igal son of Nathan of Zobah,   Bani the Gadite,

verses.kjv: Zelek the Ammonite, Naharai the Beerothite, armourbearer to Joab the son of Zeruiah,
verses.bsb: Zelek the Ammonite,   Naharai the Beerothite, the armor-bearer of Joab son of Zeruiah,

verses.kjv: Ira an Ithrite, Gareb an Ithrite,
verses.bsb: Ira the Ithrite,   Gareb the Ithrite,

verses.kjv: Uriah the Hittite: thirty and seven in all.
verses.bsb: and Uriah the Hittite.   There were thirty-seven in all.

verses.kjv: AND again the anger of the Lord was kindled against Israel, and he moved David against them to say, Go, number Israel and Judah.
verses.bsb: Again the anger of the LORD burned against Israel, and He stirred up David against them, saying, “Go and take a census of Israel and Judah.”

verses.kjv: For the king said to Joab the captain of the host, which was with him, Go now through all the tribes of Israel, from Dan even to Beer-sheba, and number ye the people, that I may know the number of the people.
verses.bsb: So the king said to Joab the commander of his army, who was with him, “Go now throughout the tribes of Israel from Dan to Beersheba and register the troops, so that I may know their number.”

verses.kjv: And Joab said unto the king, Now the Lord thy God add unto the people, how many soever they be, an hundredfold, and that the eyes of my lord the king may see it: but why doth my lord the king delight in this thing?
verses.bsb: But Joab replied to the king, “May the LORD your God multiply the troops a hundred times over, and may the eyes of my lord the king see it. But why does my lord the king want to do such a thing?”

verses.kjv: Notwithstanding the king’s word prevailed against Joab, and against the captains of the host. And Joab and the captains of the host went out from the presence of the king, to number the people of Israel.
verses.bsb: Nevertheless, the king’s word prevailed against Joab and against the commanders of the army. So Joab and the commanders of the army departed from the presence of the king to count the troops of Israel.

verses.kjv: ¶ And they passed over Jordan, and pitched in Aroer, on the right side of the city that lieth in the midst of the river of Gad, and toward Jazer:
verses.bsb: They crossed the Jordan and camped near Aroer, south of the town in the middle of the valley, and proceeded toward Gad and Jazer.

verses.kjv: Then they came to Gilead, and to the land of Tahtim-hodshi; and they came to Dan-jaan, and about to Zidon,
verses.bsb: Then they went to Gilead and the land of Tahtim-hodshi, and on to Dan-jaan and around to Sidon.

verses.kjv: And came to the strong hold of Tyre, and to all the cities of the Hivites, and of the Canaanites: and they went out to the south of Judah, even to Beer-sheba.
verses.bsb: They went toward the fortress of Tyre and all the cities of the Hivites and Canaanites. Finally, they went on to the Negev of Judah, to Beersheba.

verses.kjv: So when they had gone through all the land, they came to Jerusalem at the end of nine months and twenty days.
verses.bsb: At the end of nine months and twenty days, having gone through the whole land, they returned to Jerusalem.

verses.kjv: And Joab gave up the sum of the number of the people unto the king: and there were in Israel eight hundred thousand valiant men that drew the sword; and the men of Judah were five hundred thousand men.
verses.bsb: And Joab reported to the king the total number of the troops. In Israel there were 800,000 men of valor who drew the sword, and in Judah there were 500,000.

verses.kjv: ¶ And David’s heart smote him after that he had numbered the people. And David said unto the Lord, I have sinned greatly in that I have done: and now, I beseech thee, O Lord, take away the iniquity of thy servant; for I have done very foolishly.
verses.bsb: After David had numbered the troops, his conscience was stricken and he said to the LORD, “I have sinned greatly in what I have done. Now, O LORD, I beg You to take away the iniquity of Your servant, for I have acted very foolishly.”

verses.kjv: For when David was up in the morning, the word of the Lord came unto the prophet Gad, David’s seer, saying,
verses.bsb: When David got up in the morning, a revelation from the LORD had come to Gad the prophet, David’s seer:

verses.kjv: Go and say unto David, Thus saith the Lord, I offer thee three things; choose thee one of them, that I may do it unto thee.
verses.bsb: “Go and tell David that this is what the LORD says: ‘I am offering you three options. Choose one of them, and I will carry it out against you.’”

verses.kjv: So Gad came to David, and told him, and said unto him, Shall seven years of famine come unto thee in thy land? or wilt thou flee three months before thine enemies, while they pursue thee? or that there be three days’ pestilence in thy land? now advise, and see what answer I shall return to him that sent me.
verses.bsb: So Gad went and said to David, “Do you choose to endure three years of famine in your land, three months of fleeing the pursuit of your enemies, or three days of plague upon your land? Now then, think it over and decide how I should reply to Him who sent me.”

verses.kjv: And David said unto Gad, I am in a great strait: let us fall now into the hand of the Lord; for his mercies are great: and let me not fall into the hand of man.
verses.bsb: David answered Gad, “I am deeply distressed. Please, let us fall into the hand of the LORD, for His mercies are great; but do not let me fall into the hands of men.”

verses.kjv: ¶ So the Lord sent a pestilence upon Israel from the morning even to the time appointed: and there died of the people from Dan even to Beer-sheba seventy thousand men.
verses.bsb: So the LORD sent a plague upon Israel from that morning until the appointed time, and seventy thousand of the people from Dan to Beersheba died.

verses.kjv: And when the angel stretched out his hand upon Jerusalem to destroy it, the Lord repented him of the evil, and said to the angel that destroyed the people, It is enough: stay now thine hand. And the angel of the Lord was by the threshingplace of Araunah the Jebusite.
verses.bsb: But when the angel stretched out his hand to destroy Jerusalem, the LORD relented from the calamity and said to the angel who was destroying the people, “Enough! Withdraw your hand now!” At that time the angel of the LORD was by the threshing floor of Araunah the Jebusite.

verses.kjv: And David spake unto the Lord when he saw the angel that smote the people, and said, Lo, I have sinned, and I have done wickedly: but these sheep, what have they done? let thine hand, I pray thee, be against me, and against my father’s house.
verses.bsb: When David saw the angel striking down the people, he said to the LORD, “Surely I, the shepherd, have sinned and acted wickedly. But these sheep, what have they done? Please, let Your hand fall upon me and my father’s house.”

verses.kjv: ¶ And Gad came that day to David, and said unto him, Go up, rear an altar unto the Lord in the threshingfloor of Araunah the Jebusite.
verses.bsb: And that day Gad came to David and said to him, “Go up and build an altar to the LORD on the threshing floor of Araunah the Jebusite.”

verses.kjv: And David, according to the saying of Gad, went up as the Lord commanded.
verses.bsb: So David went up at the word of Gad, just as the LORD had commanded.

verses.kjv: And Araunah looked, and saw the king and his servants coming on toward him: and Araunah went out, and bowed himself before the king on his face upon the ground.
verses.bsb: When Araunah looked out and saw the king and his servants coming toward him, he went out and bowed facedown before the king.

verses.kjv: And Araunah said, Wherefore is my lord the king come to his servant? And David said, To buy the threshingfloor of thee, to build an altar unto the Lord, that the plague may be stayed from the people.
verses.bsb: “Why has my lord the king come to his servant?” Araunah said.  “To buy your threshing floor,” David replied, “that I may build an altar to the LORD, so that the plague upon the people may be halted.”

verses.kjv: And Araunah said unto David, Let my lord the king take and offer up what seemeth good unto him: behold, here be oxen for burnt sacrifice, and threshing instruments and other instruments of the oxen for wood.
verses.bsb: Araunah said to David, “My lord the king may take whatever seems good and offer it up. Here are the oxen for a burnt offering and the threshing sledges and ox yokes for the wood.

verses.kjv: All these things did Araunah, as a king, give unto the king. And Araunah said unto the king, The Lord thy God accept thee.
verses.bsb: O king, Araunah gives all these to the king.” He also said to the king, “May the LORD your God accept you.”

verses.kjv: And the king said unto Araunah, Nay; but I will surely buy it of thee at a price: neither will I offer burnt offerings unto the Lord my God of that which doth cost me nothing. So David bought the threshingfloor and the oxen for fifty shekels of silver.
verses.bsb: “No,” replied the king, “I insist on paying a price, for I will not offer to the LORD my God burnt offerings that cost me nothing.”  So David bought the threshing floor and the oxen for fifty shekels of silver.

verses.kjv: And David built there an altar unto the Lord, and offered burnt offerings and peace offerings. So the Lord was intreated for the land, and the plague was stayed from Israel.
verses.bsb: And there he built an altar to the LORD and offered burnt offerings and peace offerings.  Then the LORD answered the prayers on behalf of the land, and the plague upon Israel was halted.
